133741.explanation: Residents must be seriously involved in decisions that affect their living environment. We believe in participation through neighborhood meetings, citizen panels, and other forms of consultation. A referendum can be used additionally, provided it is carefully prepared and has clear safeguards.

133742.explanation: The property tax (ozb) must be distributed more fairly. Residents with low incomes should be spared to keep housing affordable. At the same time, the municipality must maintain sufficient resources to invest in good facilities and livable neighborhoods that benefit all residents.

133743.explanation: Residents know best what their neighborhood needs. Therefore, we want to give neighborhood and village councils a role in plans affecting their living environment. Not just informing, but truly deciding together. This way we make political choices that align with the diversity of Zaanstad and have broad support.

133746.explanation: Ground lease should not be a source of uncertainty or financial stress. By giving residents the option to buy off ground lease affordably, we ensure housing security and predictable costs. At the same time, we keep an eye on affordability and equal treatment.

133747.explanation: We want to provide space for companies that operate sustainably, cleanly, and with a future-oriented approach. Existing industrial sites should be better utilized and made more sustainable before new ones are developed. At the same time, space is scarce, and we prioritize housing and quality of life.

133748.explanation: A healthy living environment is a fundamental right. Polluting companies must be held accountable and, if necessary, addressed. Stricter enforcement protects residents, nature, and workers and ensures fair competition for companies that do operate sustainably and with a human focus.

133749.explanation: Nature in and around Zaanstad is under pressure and must be protected. Business growth must not come at the expense of green areas and nature but should take place within existing industrial sites through smarter and more sustainable use of space.

133750.explanation: Everyone urgently needing housing deserves a fair chance. We find it important that homes are fairly allocated based on housing need. Status holders also have the right to good accommodation. We focus on sufficient affordable housing for all groups.

133751.explanation: Zaanstad takes its responsibility in accommodating refugees, doing so out of solidarity and humanity. The municipality shelters a large number of refugees, and we think it is time for other municipalities to take their responsibility as well.

133752.explanation: Waste separation must remain easy and affordable for everyone. Paying per use disproportionately affects people with low incomes and increases the chance of littering and illegal dumping.

133753.explanation: Zaanstad wants a future-proof and healthy economy. This means new companies must contribute to sustainability, a clean living environment, and good working conditions. Companies that pollute or do not take responsibility for people and the environment do not fit in.

133754.explanation: The energy transition requires additional space for generating sustainable energy. Solar panels on farmland can play a role, provided this is done carefully and the landscape is respected.

133756.explanation: The climate crisis requires acceleration. The sooner we invest in sustainability, the greater the benefits for the climate, living environment, and residents’ finances. We want to approach this socially and fairly, with extra attention for households that cannot bear the costs themselves.

133757.explanation: To combat climate change and keep energy bills affordable, all homes must eventually disconnect from gas. We ensure that this transition is social and feasible, with extra support for low-income households and homes that are difficult to make sustainable.

133758.explanation: Wind energy is indispensable for sustainable energy supply. New wind turbines must be integrated carefully, with attention to landscape, nature, and neighbors. Local ownership and participation are important so that residents benefit from the proceeds.

133759.explanation: The polders around Assendelft and Westzaan are valuable landscapes. New high-voltage power lines must be avoided there as much as possible. Alternatives, such as infrastructure bundling or underground solutions, are preferred to protect nature and quality of life.

133760.explanation: Night flights cause serious noise nuisance and health problems for nearby residents. Zaanstad must actively work together with other municipalities to reduce or stop the number of night flights. The health and nighttime rest of residents must weigh more heavily than economic interests.

133761.explanation: Sports clubs play an indispensable role in health, social interaction, and cohesion. When clubs face financial difficulties, it can have major consequences for neighborhoods and youth. Therefore, the municipality must intervene where necessary so that sports remain accessible to everyone.

133763.explanation: This can enhance culture, social interaction, and liveliness, provided it is well integrated. A permanent site prevents temporary locations and limits nuisance in neighborhoods. Good accessibility and early involvement of nearby residents are essential; it should not come at the expense of peace or nature.

133764.explanation: We find it important that residents can exercise and be active in their own neighborhoods. Outdoor sports contribute to health, social interaction, and a pleasant neighborhood.

133765.explanation: The municipality should contribute to safe, accessible, and sustainable sports facilities because this makes us all healthier. It also helps people meet each other, making the neighborhood safer and more enjoyable.

133766.explanation: Preserving heritage and characteristic buildings is important because they contribute to the identity and history of our city. It is good to carefully consider which buildings deserve protection, balancing historical value, neighborhood importance, and future viability.

133767.explanation: Safety in the neighborhood starts with visibility and presence. Extra community enforcement officers provide more supervision, faster enforcement, and greater approachability in the neighborhood. They can detect problems early, act to de-escalate, and contribute to safer streets.

133768.explanation: Camera surveillance can contribute to safety, especially in places with structural nuisance or crime. However, it is not an end in itself. Cameras must be used carefully and purposefully, with clear privacy safeguards and always in combination with human supervision.

133770.explanation: The increasing possession of knives among youth is worrying and requires prevention. Education makes young people aware of risks and consequences. Combined with youth work, education, and parents, this helps prevent violence.

133771.explanation: Nuisance among youth often indicates underlying problems such as lack of perspective or guidance. By investing in youth work, sports, culture, and support, nuisance can be structurally reduced and young people gain more opportunities for positive development.

133772.explanation: The housing shortage in Zaanstad is severe. Scaling back building plans only worsens this problem. New homes must be carefully integrated, with attention to greenery, facilities, and accessibility.

133773.explanation: For people with middle incomes, it is increasingly difficult to find suitable housing. By focusing more on mid-priced rental and owner-occupied homes in new construction, we improve housing market flow and prevent this group from falling between the cracks.

133774.explanation: Greening helps against heat stress, flooding, and contributes to biodiversity. By financially supporting residents to green their gardens, the municipality stimulates climate adaptation at neighborhood level and makes the living environment more attractive and healthier.

133775.explanation: Demolition and new construction may be necessary when buildings are no longer technically or energy-wise improvable. However, reuse and renovation must always be examined first. New construction must be sustainable, future-proof, and fitting in the environment.

133776.explanation: A neighborhood functions well only if basic amenities are nearby. This makes it a pleasant neighborhood and a nice place to live. New districts must therefore be developed integrally, with sufficient facilities from the start.

133777.explanation: Unrestricted subdivision of homes can lead to nuisance, insecurity, and displacement of regular households. The municipality must set and enforce clear rules to preserve living quality and prevent abuses.

133778.explanation: It is important that people who have lived in Zaanstad for a long time or have a strong bond with it keep prospects for affordable housing in their own municipality. At the same time, it remains important to make room for people who urgently need housing.

133779.explanation: The housing shortage mainly affects people with low incomes. By maintaining at least 40% social rental housing, Zaanstad prevents becoming unaffordable for large groups of residents. This contributes to mixed neighborhoods and social cohesion.

133780.explanation: A homeownership obligation prevents speculation and ensures that affordable homes remain available for first-time buyers and families, rather than investors. This helps make the housing market fairer.

133781.explanation: By smartly combining living and working, the housing shortage can be addressed without extra pressure on the landscape. However, residential and work environments must be well separated where necessary and the quality of life for residents must be guaranteed.

133782.explanation: The villages border nature reserves that must be protected. To create sufficient housing, expansion is sometimes unavoidable, but the principle should be not to do this.

133783.explanation: Poverty obstructs daily life and participation in society. Investing in poverty reduction increases livelihood security and prevents stress and debts. By supporting incomes up to 130% of the subsistence minimum, we prevent people from falling through the cracks. Prevention is cheaper.

133784.explanation: People on welfare often want to participate but do not always have the immediate means. Mandatory work obligations often backfire and increase stress and mistrust.

133785.explanation: The accessibility of Wormerveer and Krommenie is under pressure. An additional crossing over the Zaan can contribute to better traffic flow and increased safety. At the same time, such an intervention must be carefully studied, with attention to livability, nature, and noise pollution.

133786.explanation: Good accessibility must not stop after midnight. For people working in healthcare, hospitality, or industry, reliable public transport is essential. A 24-hour opening of the bus bridge contributes to accessibility, safety, and a well-functioning region.

133787.explanation: New roads often cause more traffic and extra emissions, while space is limited. Therefore, focus should be on better public transport, good cycling connections, and smart mobility solutions. This keeps the city accessible, healthy, and pleasant to live in.

133788.explanation: The railway line forms a physical and mental barrier in the city. Putting it underground can reduce noise pollution, increase safety, and provide space for housing and greenery. Although costly, this can bring great long-term benefits for livability and cohesion in Zaanstad.

133790.explanation: A night train improves accessibility for people who work or go out in the evening and night. It increases safety, reduces car traffic, and aligns with a sustainable mobility vision.

133791.explanation: The construction of the A8–A9 harms nature, living environment, and the health of local residents. It is an expensive project with little effect on accessibility. We prefer better public transport, smart traffic measures, and investments in a healthy living environment.

133792.explanation: The Vlinderbrug is intended for slow traffic and forms a safe connection for cyclists and pedestrians. Opening it to cars would harm traffic safety and reduce livability in surrounding neighborhoods. We consciously choose safe and car-reduced connections.

133793.explanation: Residents must be able to rely on parking their car near their home. For many people-such as families, elderly, and those dependent on a car for work or care-sufficient parking space is essential.

133794.explanation: Paid parking is an important tool to fairly allocate public space and limit nuisance. Too low fees lead to extra traffic and parking pressure. However, the system must be socially just, with attention to residents, people with disabilities, and lower incomes.

133795.explanation: Air pollution harms health, especially of children and elderly. An environmental zone contributes to cleaner air and a healthier living environment. By banning polluting vehicles while supporting the transition to cleaner mobility, we make Zaanstad future-proof.

133796.explanation: Lower speeds lead to fewer traffic accidents, less noise, and a more pleasant living environment. 30 km/h is already the standard in many cities and aligns with the goal of a safe, pleasant residential area.

133797.explanation: Young people need safe and accessible places to meet each other. This prevents nuisance, increases social cohesion, and provides room for development. Municipalities must actively support these places, together with the youth themselves.

133798.explanation: Care close to home is essential, especially with an aging population. Care neighborhood centers make it possible to combine support, meeting, and care and strengthen social cohesion in villages and neighborhoods.

133799.explanation: Informal caregivers provide indispensable care that relieves much pressure from professional care institutions. Higher appreciation is a form of recognition for their efforts and helps them continue that care.

133800.explanation: Good preparation for pregnancy and parenthood contributes to healthy starting opportunities for children. By offering courses for free, we lower barriers and invest in preventive healthcare.

133801.explanation: Swimming safety is essential in a water-rich country like the Netherlands. Every child must learn to swim, regardless of parents' income. Free swimming lessons increase equal opportunities and prevent drowning accidents.

133741.explanation: Clearer rules must be introduced to prevent misuse of the instrument. Remember, a referendum costs 400,000 euros; you don't do that every week.

133742.explanation: If possible, every tax should be reduced. Taxes currently levied must cover costs and should not be used to fill holes in the budget.

133743.explanation: We have a participation regulation in Zaanstad that states how and when residents can participate. If a village council is an addition to that, we support it.

133746.explanation: Certainly. The POV has fought hard for years for a realistic buyout sum. This must be fully clear to homeowners within the next four years.

133747.explanation: Yes and no. Business parks where living makes more sense can be transformed. Other business parks can be expanded and living excluded. This creates clarity for entrepreneurs and residents.

133748.explanation: Yes, and stricter measures are needed to punish violations of environmental rules. Cooperation with the Environmental Service must also be improved.

133749.explanation: Only if possible; the POV prefers building homes on the edges of the city near nature. If necessary, building homes may come at the expense of nature edges.

133750.explanation: No, they should not. The national government must lift the housing task. It cannot be that residents of Zaanstad are put at the back of the queue because of that.

133751.explanation: No, definitely not. Zaanstad must urge the national government to take other measures to reduce the number of incoming refugees. Zaanstad must tell the national government that the maximum term for shelter is also maximum.

133752.explanation: Here we are not yet sure if this is the cheapest solution for the residents. We favor a differentiation of costs per household. It is absurd that one person pays as much as a family of four.

133753.explanation: Disagree, but we do think sustainable working is important. If the condition is as stated, companies will not be willing to establish themselves in Zaanstad.

133754.explanation: Solar panels then cover the ground underneath and nothing will grow anymore. A solution must be found that does not damage nature.

133756.explanation: Disagree because this is simply an unfeasible task.

133757.explanation: Disagree, there must always be electricity because gas costs have risen sharply again recently. However, good agreements must be made about the feasibility of a district heating network.

133758.explanation: Wind turbines run on subsidies. Many investors and energy companies quit due to high costs and low returns. No new wind turbines should be added and the old ones should not be replaced.

133759.explanation: Certainly. A good solution must be found to adjust the route so that the pipeline can go underground.

133760.explanation: Schiphol is a major employer in the region. The national government has still not provided an alternative. Also, increasingly more silent airplanes are appearing and therefore the nuisance will decrease.

133761.explanation: Sports clubs can be supported when major investments need to be made. The clubs operate on membership fees and are not property of the municipality. However, it is important to help maintain them.

133763.explanation: Completely agree. Over the past four years, we have been able to preserve the grounds at the Omzoom until 2028 for the neighborhood. Unfortunately, this is temporary and must become permanent. Additionally, we need to consider how many events the neighborhood can handle.

133764.explanation: Agreed. Where possible, this could be realized. Residents can suggest initiatives and the municipality can facilitate them. This way, uncontrolled growth is prevented.

133765.explanation: The municipality can support where possible, but it is not the financier of sports clubs. It is important to collectively determine where the possibilities lie. The clubs also collect membership fees and need to be self-sufficient.

133766.explanation: No, definitely not. Only if it has been demonstrated that a building deserves a special monument status should this be applied. Remember, heritage costs a lot of money to maintain.

133767.explanation: Agreed. Zaanstad is growing, which means the city must grow in the number of enforcement officers. In the past, many rules were devised without considering the need for staff to perform controls.

133768.explanation: Agreed. In places where it is necessary and adds value. There are often calls for Zaanstad to be safer, but few parties want to invest in it. Zaanstad must be safe for everyone.

133770.explanation: Definitely, and this is already happening in schools. We must invest in the projects currently running and not withdraw. Safety initiatives require sustained effort.

133771.explanation: Certainly. The involvement of youth work in Poelenburg this past New Year's Eve demonstrated the importance of good youth work. We must create support for this throughout Zaanstad.

133772.explanation: Disagree! There is a housing shortage, which means we need to keep building. However, we must better consider where we build and how to provide access to new neighborhoods. This also applies to parking problems.

133773.explanation: Certainly. The solution to the bottleneck in housing flow lies in creating mid-range rental from 900 euros. The solution is not adding more social housing. We also need to build more for seniors who want to move on, including in the home ownership market.

133774.explanation: We definitely disagree; it must remain a responsibility of the residents. Stop patronizing.

133775.explanation: Without demolition, no renewal. If demolition is not allowed, we cannot add large numbers of homes while facing a housing crisis.

133776.explanation: This is an unfeasible task. However, we must better identify where these facilities are needed and act accordingly. Not wait for problems to arise but anticipate them.

133777.explanation: Zaanstad already does this. A permit is required to convert a home into multiple rooms. This is done to prevent nuisance from room rentals. If possible, we should allow it.

133778.explanation: Agreed. Zaanstad should not become the dumping ground for the Amsterdam Metropolitan Region. Surrounding municipalities must also build more than they currently do, or else we should consider leaving the housing network.

133779.explanation: Strongly disagree. 30% is sufficient. There is a need for mid-range rental to facilitate movement out of social housing.

133780.explanation: Agreed. This rule should apply throughout Zaanstad. The home may only be sublet to family members. Such subletting should require a permit.

133781.explanation: There are some industrial sites where we do not enforce this, which is important for heavier industry. We must of course continue to enable these.

133782.explanation: If we want to relieve the inner cities, we must look to the outer edges of neighborhoods. We need to carefully assess how many homes are possible there, but we must make it possible. The province also has a facilitating role in this.

133783.explanation: Yes, otherwise the efforts of recent years have been for nothing.

133784.explanation: Certainly, we have little to add. Except when it can be demonstrably shown that it is physically or mentally impossible.

133785.explanation: The current Zaan bridge cost 32 million and functions well. Another bridge over the Zaan is unnecessary!

133786.explanation: The decision to open the bridge has already been made and must not be reversed. The bridge must open as soon as possible after the safety measures in Westerkoog are completed.

133787.explanation: Then access to all neighborhoods and villages in Zaanstad is at risk. We should even consider building a little more to ensure good traffic flow.

133788.explanation: If possible, this is our preference, but the cost is enormous.

133790.explanation: Definitely not; high costs and little effect. We would be better off investing this money in a good bus connection that runs every night.

133791.explanation: Yes, absolutely! The A8/A9 must be constructed. This connection also adds value to the northern part of North Holland. The connection is important for future housing as well.

133792.explanation: Certainly, and there should be an exit on Thorbeckeweg to directly connect to the neighborhood. This will relieve the city center and prevent traffic jams at Vijfhoek.

133793.explanation: No, definitely not! Parking should be solved within residential areas; this can be done well within the inner city. It also provides clarity to developers.

133794.explanation: Agreed. The agreement is that taxes must be cost-covering and not used to fill budget gaps. Where possible, rates should be lowered.

133795.explanation: As far as we are concerned, this is an undesirable development. Residents and entrepreneurs are unnecessarily burdened with costs and the business climate deteriorates significantly.

133796.explanation: The maximum speed is already legally set by the national government. We do not need to implement additional measures in our municipality.

133797.explanation: Only if it adds value. Saendelft also had a nice hangout spot, which has since been demolished. We would rather invest in youth work.

133798.explanation: Every neighborhood should have its own community center. Thanks to the POV, we have maintained 19 community centers in the past four years without budget cuts. A new community center scheme will make expansion possible.

133799.explanation: People benefit more from respite care than money.

133800.explanation: Having children remains an individual responsibility; nonsense statement.

133801.explanation: Since 2010, the POV has supported free school swimming. Zaanstad is rich in water; children must be able to swim.

133741.explanation: The Zaan VVD believes that residents should be able to vote every four years for who represents them and makes decisions on their behalf for the municipality.

133742.explanation: The Zaan VVD believes homeowners already pay enough, so if the property tax can be lowered, it should be lowered.

133743.explanation: The Zaanse VVD considers resident participation very important, so everyone may have a say, but not everyone can decide.

133746.explanation: The Zaanse VVD believes that ground lease should be able to be bought off at a fair price.

133747.explanation: The Zaanse VVD finds it important that people can live, live their lives, and work in the municipality. Business parks are necessary for this mix.

133748.explanation: The Zaanse VVD believes that everyone must follow the rules, including businesses.

133749.explanation: The Zaanse VVD finds it important that people can live, live their lives, and work in the municipality. Business parks are indeed necessary, and having them on the edges of the municipality is a good option.

133750.explanation: The Zaanse VVD believes that young people from Zaanstad should be given priority for social rental housing.

133751.explanation: The Zaanse VVD feels that Zaanstad already does more than enough; doing even more than the legal requirement comes at the expense of our own residents.

133752.explanation: The Zaanse VVD believes that waste collection is a primary task of the municipality and should not involve paying per pickup.

133753.explanation: The Zaanse VVD welcomes all entrepreneurs who contribute to Zaanstad; sometimes, due to circumstances, these entrepreneurs cannot yet operate sustainably.

133754.explanation: The Zaanse VVD believes the electricity shortage must be resolved as quickly as possible; if this helps, we support it.

133756.explanation: In Zaanstad, we are already doing more than enough to meet the climate agreement; doing even more harms (the finances of) our residents.

133757.explanation: If it is feasible, then it can be done; if not, we shouldn’t hold on to a strict deadline.

133758.explanation: Many wind turbines are already being built; that is all the space there is.

133759.explanation: The Zaanse VVD believes the electricity shortage must be solved as soon as possible. Unfortunately, these kinds of measures are part of that.

133760.explanation: Where night flights can end, they should end. In practice, mostly freight and holiday flights fly at night. These can be moved to daytime.

133761.explanation: Sports are extremely important; we must not lose that in Zaanstad.

133763.explanation: The Zaanse VVD believes that living, working, and living life must be possible in Zaanstad. An event terrain fits well with that.

133764.explanation: Sports are extremely important; we must not lose that in Zaanstad, and outdoor sports facilities contribute to encounters among our residents.

133765.explanation: The clubhouses are often already of good quality.

133766.explanation: People must be able to live in Zaanstad! Some buildings are worth preserving, but many could make way for new construction and homes that meet the requirements of 2026.

133767.explanation: Safety is crucial to feeling comfortable in your own municipality.

133768.explanation: Cameras often lead to a greater sense of safety because actual perpetrators are caught with them!

133770.explanation: The municipality must confiscate those weapons and fine the parents of these children.

133772.explanation: In Zaanstad, building must be for our residents.

133773.explanation: Building must be for everyone, including this group. Construction for this group creates flow from the social rental sector, so less needs to be built in this category since Zaanstad already has a high number of social rental homes.

133774.explanation: Everyone should be free to arrange their garden however they want.

133775.explanation: If the plan is an improvement to the public space and the building has no historical value, demolition and new construction should certainly be possible. This is often an opportunity to make the location meet the requirements of 2026.

133776.explanation: A pleasant neighborhood is more than just housing.

133777.explanation: It is proven that subdividing houses causes problems such as illegal housing of labor migrants and parking nuisance because the street is not designed for the number of vehicles.

133778.explanation: Zaanstad fundamentally builds for its own residents.

133779.explanation: There are enough social rental homes in Zaanstad; building in the middle-priced rental and owner-occupied sectors is the solution to the long waiting lists for social rental housing.

133780.explanation: Buying a home as an investment for old age or your child should be perfectly fine. The slumlords who buy homes and rent them out in poor condition by the dozens must be tackled as they disrupt the housing market.

133781.explanation: If there is space for combining living and working, that possibility should exist. It must not come at the expense of our businesses.

133782.explanation: The Zaanse VVD considers it important that living, working, and living life is possible in the municipality. Because we don’t only need apartments in Zaanstad, building at the edges of the municipality is a good option.

133783.explanation: The municipality must better allocate the budget (which has not been used year after year).

133784.explanation: The Zaanse VVD believes it is normal to work. If that’s temporarily not possible, you receive social assistance, and the municipality may expect something in return.

133785.explanation: The Zaan region is getting congested; an extra bridge helps to prevent that.

133786.explanation: Residents were already promised during the construction of Westerwatering that the bus bridge would open. Better open yesterday than today.

133787.explanation: Zaanstad is growing, and so is the number of cars; therefore, more roads are extremely important.

133788.explanation: Research shows that this is technically not feasible.

133790.explanation: Research shows that this costs €30 per traveler in subsidies!

133791.explanation: The road is extremely important for our residents and our accessibility.

133792.explanation: A quick trip from one side to the other side of the city over a bridge that already exists is a good idea.

133793.explanation: There is a severe shortage of parking spaces, with no neighborhood in Zaanstad exempted.

133794.explanation: Paid parking should only cover costs. The Zaanse VVD believes that the municipality should not make a profit from it.

133795.explanation: Supplying shops and restaurants is more important.

133796.explanation: Only in places where it is necessary for traffic safety.

133797.explanation: A meeting place has been proven to reduce nuisance.

133798.explanation: A sympathetic idea, but financially unfeasible.

133799.explanation: The increase from 50 to 140 does not give more appreciation for the invaluable value of this commitment.

133800.explanation: Residents of Zaanstad who expect a child can obtain this information themselves and through various other channels.

133801.explanation: We see the importance of sports and exercise but believe that parents are primarily responsible for teaching their children to swim.

133741.explanation: Agree. Democracy is not just voting once every four years, but a continuous process of representation.

133742.explanation: Agree. Municipal taxes cannot keep rising continuously to cover financial gaps. Other measures are needed, such as a critical look at the municipality's spending patterns.

133743.explanation: Agree. By abolishing the neighborhood and village councils, the distance between the municipality and residents has further increased. This should be reversed.

133746.explanation: Agree. The current system is demonstrably opaque and its implementation has caused great harm to stakeholders.

133747.explanation: Disagree. A clear choice must be made between living and working and their distribution, without both obstructing each other. Just as it was before.

133748.explanation: Disagree. There is no evidence that more is needed than what already happens through permitting, Environmental Services, or enforcement.

133749.explanation: Disagree. A clear choice must be made between living and working and their distribution, without both obstructing each other. Just as it was before.

133750.explanation: Disagree. Real participation in society also means accepting the rules that apply to everyone. Moreover, positive discrimination is still discrimination.

133751.explanation: Agree. Zaanstad has enough social challenges among its own residents.

133752.explanation: Disagree. The municipality should look for alternatives for waste processing that are more efficient and thus cheaper in practice, such as post-separation.

133753.explanation: Disagree. Moreover, the definition of what is sustainable is arbitrary, as we see with biomass.

133754.explanation: Disagree. Solar panels are landscape pollution and limit farmers in their operations.

133756.explanation: Disagree. Zaanstad does not need to be climate neutral ever. Zaanstad's effect on the overall global picture in this issue is negligible.

133757.explanation: Disagree. Especially given the upcoming energy crisis, it is only good if gas remains an alternative.

133758.explanation: Disagree. Wind turbines are landscape pollution and run on subsidies.

133759.explanation: Agree. The masts are landscape pollution and limit farmers in their operations. Moreover, there are questions about the health of residents living relatively close to the masts, which has consequences for future housing development.

133760.explanation: Agree. It is not wrong to continuously draw attention to the nuisance that some of our residents continue to experience.

133761.explanation: Agree. Sports clubs are an important provision for the health and well-being of our residents and we therefore see it as the municipality's task to care for them, also financially.

133763.explanation: Disagree. Considering all spatial challenges within the municipal borders, this is unrealistic and moreover a waste of space.

133764.explanation: Agree. Public spaces can become more lively and multifunctional. This is also good for social safety.

133765.explanation: Agree. Sport may cost something as a core task, especially to prevent forced relocations and mergers, so that more individuality is preserved.

133766.explanation: Disagree. Monuments are not an end in themselves but a means to preserve what has important cultural-historical value.

133767.explanation: Agree, although more enforcement officers do not necessarily increase safety, at least not by themselves. Other measures are also needed.

133768.explanation: Disagree. Monitoring citizens' private lives is at least as important as ensuring safety. Moreover, you only achieve greater safety with camera surveillance if it is actively monitored, which requires a lot of money.

133770.explanation: Agree, although this is not just the municipality's task but also that of caregivers and security services. Ultimately, it is a societal problem.

133771.explanation: Disagree. The effort in Zaandam-East is substantial, also financially, and its results must first be visible to know what is effective and what is not. Moreover, many other neighborhoods experience serious youth nuisance.

133772.explanation: Agree. Urban densification must be considered across the entire municipality and per area, realistically. In addition, the plans to build 20,000 homes must be reviewed in relation to the level of facilities and the protection of business activities in Zaanstad.

133773.explanation: Disagree. Zaanstad has a relatively large share of low incomes, which puts additional pressure on social provisions. A better balance in the financial capacity of residents has a positive effect on this.

133774.explanation: Disagree. This is not a task for the municipality and moreover, much is already being done to actively highlight the benefits of greening gardens. Also, through adjustments in the APV, greening could be enforced to some degree without extra costs for the municipality.

133775.explanation: Agree, while maintaining attention to truly worthy or monumental buildings. The municipality could contribute to this through regulations or subsidies.

133776.explanation: Agree. This was also common practice in the 1960s and 1970s and leads to greater involvement in one's own neighborhood or district, more liveliness, and less nuisance due to increased social control.

133777.explanation: Agree. Rooming often causes extra nuisance in neighborhoods and districts.

133778.explanation: Agree. Currently, construction mainly takes place for people from outside the municipality, and Zaanstad absorbs a large part of the regional demand.

133779.explanation: Disagree. Zaanstad has a relatively large share of low incomes, which puts additional pressure on social provisions. A better balance in the financial capacity of residents has a positive effect on this.

133780.explanation: Disagree. The government has no say in this.

133781.explanation: Disagree, unless it concerns business operations subject to different standards or the residences also have a different function than just housing, for example combining supervision of the industrial area with living.

133782.explanation: Disagree, it is important to protect our peat meadow areas.

133783.explanation: Disagree, there is no evidence that this is necessary and it also costs a lot of extra money for which the municipality is not compensated.

133784.explanation: Agree. This provision is a safety net aimed at temporarily compensating for what one cannot manage at the moment. A counter-performance is a good step towards new work to gain work experience or contribute socially.

133785.explanation: Agree. There must be a connection to the Willis plan in Krommenie.

133786.explanation: Agree, given the environmental impact caused by detouring.

133787.explanation: Disagree. The infrastructure in Zaanstad shows room for improvement at various points. Moreover, the number of cars will not decrease as the population increases.

133788.explanation: Agree. We support partially tunneling the Zaanlijn. This improves traffic safety, gives space back to Zaanstad for greenery and housing, and reduces noise nuisance.

133790.explanation: Agree, this is also part of a growing and relatively large city.

133791.explanation: Agree, although the project has been definitively cancelled. Therefore, focus should now be on alternatives.

133792.explanation: Agree, traffic flow management in Zaandam-South would improve significantly and it would prevent detours.

133793.explanation: Disagree. Reducing parking spaces is unrealistic because cars do not simply disappear.

133794.explanation: Agree. As long as the quantity of parking facilities is so limited, it creates scarcity that is then punished with high parking costs.

133795.explanation: Disagree. This has very negative effects on entrepreneurs' business operations.

133796.explanation: Disagree. Lower speeds do not automatically mean fewer accidents. The effect strongly depends on enforcement, road design, and behavior.

133797.explanation: Agree, although this depends on the composition of villages and needs within these villages. It is questionable whether a youth center is always the solution. A meeting place is.

133798.explanation: Agree, if this need is truly present. The idea behind this form of living is interesting, also given rising life expectancy.

133799.explanation: Disagree. Appreciation is different from a subsidy that aims to stimulate certain behavior or effort. This is not the case with informal care, because you are confronted with it.

133800.explanation: Disagree. A certain degree of self-reliance can be expected from residents, which generally exists. For those who need it, various forms of help and guidance are available.

133801.explanation: Agree, as part of education, as was the case in the past.

133741.explanation: There are so many important decisions that it would become too expensive and complicated if the municipal council had to organize a referendum for each. According to ROSA, residents can always request a referendum, provided it is not commercial or misleading.

133742.explanation: Important facilities for residents are financed through property tax. A reduction would mean cuts to these facilities. Zaanstad's housing costs are at the national average and ROSA finds this acceptable.

133743.explanation: ROSA has always supported neighborhood and village councils, which can have a say in decisions affecting their own living environment. The municipality should support these councils where needed.

133746.explanation: ROSA supports a leasehold system as a tool against speculation. But if residents want to acquire the land under their home, this should be arranged fairly. If it can be done advantageously, that is a bonus.

133747.explanation: We need to use existing industrial areas more intelligently, such as through dual use or combining functions.

133748.explanation: The environmental service conducts inspections on behalf of the municipality. We find it important that inspections are thorough and regular!

133749.explanation: No, absolutely not. Nature in Zaanstad is already under too much pressure.

133750.explanation: Status holders have no social safety net, unlike many others, and therefore should be given priority, just like all others who are in distress.

133751.explanation: As long as not every Dutch municipality cooperates in refugee reception, other municipalities will have to contribute extra. ROSA is proud that Zaanstad takes its responsibility in this.

133752.explanation: Zaanstad still produces far too much residual waste. To reduce this, producers must change packaging and a system must be introduced so residents who carefully separate waste pay less.

133753.explanation: As far as ROSA is concerned, companies that go above the legal sustainability requirements are especially welcome.

133754.explanation: Yes, but let's start by covering all roofs first.

133756.explanation: Regardless of agreements, we must make every effort to become climate neutral as soon as possible. By the way, the original target year was 2020...

133757.explanation: We must be gas-free as soon as possible. This is better for the environment and for the wallet.

133758.explanation: We need all sustainable energy sources. However, we would also like to see space for more traditional wind turbines, such as those realized on De Hemmes.

133759.explanation: Zaanstad has made its protest against this plan loud and clear. Unfortunately, the province has approved it; the ball is now in the hands of the national government.

133760.explanation: Unfortunately, Schiphol is so powerful that it appears to be above the law. But of course, Zaanstad must continue to exert as much pressure as possible.

133761.explanation: We see a trend in society toward more individual sports, but team sports are also important, especially for children. So, where sports clubs are viable, we should provide support where needed.

133763.explanation: It is important that events can be organized throughout Zaanstad. This must not come at the expense of nature and other functions, so multifunctional venues (in multiple places) are preferable.

133764.explanation: More and more people are engaging in individual sports. Facilities for this must be available nearby.

133765.explanation: Fortunately, we are already doing this generously.

133766.explanation: Designating monuments is a means, not an end. ROSA wants to preserve Zaanstad’s identity by continuing to tell its stories. Designating monuments and landmark objects (which do not always have to be old) helps with this.

133767.explanation: Prefer people on the streets over cameras.

133768.explanation: Prefer people on the streets over cameras. Cameras create a false sense of security.

133770.explanation: Young people face great (peer) pressure. It is good to offer counterbalance to this. Fortunately, Zaanstad already does this in many areas.

133771.explanation: A lot of money is already being spent on this. ROSA prefers preventive measures, which are much cheaper than enforcement and better help the young people themselves.

133772.explanation: ROSA has always said it does not want to build more than possible. What that means for the North will have to become clear in the (distant) future. It is not wise to present firm numbers now.

133773.explanation: The biggest demand remains affordable housing. As far as ROSA is concerned, the share of social housing in new projects may be increased from 30 to 40 percent.

133774.explanation: Years ago, a motion submitted by ROSA for this was adopted. As far as we are concerned, it should be implemented as soon as possible.

133775.explanation: Never demolish again! From the perspective of sustainability and recognizability of Zaanstad, we should preserve as many buildings as possible. Nowadays, this is usually also cheaper than demolition and new construction. This also presents beautiful challenges for architects and developers.

133776.explanation: Housing is more than a house; it is also a social living environment. Facilities and greenery are important in this regard.

133777.explanation: Converting houses into multiple rooms must meet strict rules and is not desired everywhere. But adding rooms can help people, such as students and other young people. Fortunately, Zaanstad handles this carefully.

133778.explanation: Yes, but without infringing on the rights of other urgent housing seekers.

133779.explanation: There is a great need for affordable housing.

133780.explanation: To prevent speculation with houses, owner-occupation should be mandatory where possible. Fortunately, this is already the case for cheaper housing in Zaanstad, and as far as we are concerned, this may be expanded.

133781.explanation: But only if this is possible considering environmental nuisances.

133782.explanation: Not one more street, but one more layer on top. We should leave our green space to nature.

133783.explanation: The government in The Hague is responsible for incomes. However, Zaanstad can support residents with little money in many areas, which we fortunately do. But of course, we should always strive to do more.

133784.explanation: This is already the case and this is the law.

133785.explanation: Cars do not need to be further facilitated in Zaanstad. However, we are in favor of ferry services for pedestrians and cyclists, because that is enjoyable.

133786.explanation: We have always said no, because where too many cars are the problem, car use should not be facilitated. But unfortunately, the council decided years ago by majority that the Bus Bridge must remain permanently open. So what parties think about it now is no longer relevant.

133787.explanation: In the spirit of freedom of choice, in the coming decades we must focus on facilities for slow traffic and public transport.

133788.explanation: This would make complicated railway crossing solutions unnecessary and create space for greenery and housing. The increase in train traffic made possible by this also relieves Zaanstad North of road traffic.

133790.explanation: A full-fledged nighttime train connection is highly desired.

133791.explanation: ROSA has always been a strong opponent of this destructive connection. We have always advocated for the pristine preservation of nature values and the UNESCO World Heritage Site, the Defense Line of Amsterdam. We also opposed the increase in car movements through Zaanstad (at least 30,000 extra per day!), so we are glad this plan is definitively off the table.

133792.explanation: ROSA does not see added value in this.

133793.explanation: Especially in neighborhoods with little greenery, where green spaces and playing areas for children need to be added.

133794.explanation: Our parking rates are not high compared to other cities. Furthermore, ROSA supports encouraging people to choose other means of transport.

133795.explanation: Yes, because this significantly improves air quality and reduces the greenhouse effect.

133796.explanation: This is better for safety, the environment, and livability in general. A handful of through-routes could possibly remain at 50 km per hour.

133797.explanation: Young people need a ‘home away from home’ where they can safely meet each other.

133798.explanation: A care neighborhood center is a nice facility between one’s own home and a nursing home. This allows partners, of whom one needs care, to stay together. ROSA supports small-scale housing facilities at the neighborhood level.

133799.explanation: Most caregivers benefit more from support and temporary relief than from money. So the caregiver budget can be better spent on that.

133800.explanation: As far as ROSA is concerned, this belongs in the basic health insurance. As long as that is not the case, it should be subsidized, especially for those with less financial means.

133801.explanation: Swimming lessons are essential in a water-rich environment. Not all parents can arrange this for their children, and the municipality must step in. This can be done, for example, by reintroducing school swimming.

133741.explanation: The PVV supports referenda, but only for truly important and far-reaching decisions. In Zaanstad, however, there is only the consultative referendum: an instrument without binding power that the executive board and the council can simply disregard.

133742.explanation: The PVV favors the lowest possible property tax for its residents. Due to vacancies, speculators do not pay the user portion of the property tax, causing the other residents and entrepreneurs to pay disproportionately too much. That is unjust.

133743.explanation: Citizen participation is valuable but has its limits. Council members are elected to take responsibility and make choices, not to pass on that responsibility.

133746.explanation: The leasehold system in Zaanstad is unfair. Houses on leasehold receive the same WOZ value as houses on private land, but leaseholders pay extra because of this. The PVV wants to make leasehold fair and affordable again.

133747.explanation: In principle, Zaanstad has enough business parks, but they are threatened by housing development. If that happens, it must always be compensated with new business parks.

133748.explanation: Although the municipality bears the administrative responsibility, this is the executive task of the Environmental Service.

133749.explanation: In a growing city, business activity is important, but there must be selective consideration of how this is realized. Nature must never be an obstacle to growth and livelihood security.

133750.explanation: Absolutely not; discrimination against our own residents in favor of status holders is unacceptable. Our residents must get local priority.

133751.explanation: According to the Distribution Act, we must accommodate about 800 asylum seekers, but we are currently hosting twice that number. This also means twice as much nuisance. We oppose the Distribution Act but will continue to defend the interests of Zaanstad.

133752.explanation: We already see a lot of nuisance from illegally dumped waste. This change will only make that problem bigger. We do support a differentiated rate for single-person households.

133753.explanation: What is the next step; will only sustainable residents be allowed to settle here?

133754.explanation: The PVV is critical of weather-dependent energy solutions (such as wind and solar) and is strongly against using agricultural land for these purposes. This is bad for food production and the beautiful Dutch landscape.

133756.explanation: The Zaans Climate Agreement should be shredded.

133757.explanation: The word 'must' in this question says enough; we support free choice.

133758.explanation: Wind turbines in densely populated areas cause a lot of nuisance.

133759.explanation: We oppose high-voltage pylons and prefer underground cables. If the province says this is not possible, that must be demonstrably and fairly substantiated.

133760.explanation: Schiphol enforces a night flight ban between 11:00 PM and 6:00 AM for most commercial flights, with exceptions for certain special categories such as cargo flights or emergencies.

133761.explanation: Sports clubs in Zaanstad receive subsidies for facilities, youth, and maintenance. Good governance is necessary for sports clubs to operate financially healthy.

133763.explanation: An event area in North Zaanstad is necessary and could possibly be designed for multifunctional use.

133764.explanation: In practice, these places are seldom used, often serve as hangouts for youth, and could be better designed for multifunctional use.

133765.explanation: The municipality is responsible for sports facilities and clubhouses and must ensure improvements where needed.

133766.explanation: Not every old building is a monument.

133767.explanation: Many people no longer feel safe on the streets. The municipality must do everything to improve the safety feeling of its residents.

133768.explanation: Many people no longer feel safe on the streets. The municipality must do everything to improve the safety feeling of its residents.

133770.explanation: This and that about education. Possession of stabbing weapons is simply prohibited.

133771.explanation: Youth nuisance is a serious problem and must be addressed. There are sufficient rules; what is mainly needed now is adequate enforcement, because more money alone does not provide a solution.

133772.explanation: Expansion cannot take place as long as infrastructure does not grow with the number of homes. North Zaanstad is becoming congested and must retain its character; it now seems as if building is for people from outside Zaanstad.

133773.explanation: More mid-rent housing stimulates flow and facilitates an often forgotten target group.

133774.explanation: Zaanstad poorly cares for its own public greenery and should not interfere with someone else's greenery.

133775.explanation: If a building loses its function, it must be allowed to be demolished for a new purpose, provided it is not a monument and not for speculative reasons.

133776.explanation: A growing city requires growing social facilities, such as general practitioners, childcare, and schools.

133777.explanation: There are rules against subdividing homes into multiple rooms, and these must be strictly enforced. Too many subdivided homes in a street or neighborhood disrupt the community and cause nuisance.

133778.explanation: Completely agree: the PVV wants housing-seeking Zaankanters and those with economic ties to get priority, instead of too many homes going to status holders.

133779.explanation: For a balanced city, 30% social rental housing is more than enough; more is financially not feasible and disrupts the living environment as in the Poelenburg neighborhood where over 60% of the homes are social rental.

133780.explanation: In times of scarcity, price-inflating speculation must be prevented, so owner-occupation is important.

133781.explanation: Business parks are full of activity. If housing is built there, complaints about nuisance from the existing businesses are likely.

133782.explanation: One more street is no problem, but the village character must be preserved and the infrastructure must be able to handle it.

133783.explanation: We support poverty reduction but mainly focus on good guidance to prevent poverty and problematic debts.

133784.explanation: Social work is meaningful but not at the expense of a paid job. Encouraging people to work is important; there are enough vacancies. If suitable work is not available, education or retraining is crucial so people can provide for their own income.

133785.explanation: The only connection the PVV can think of is an extra bridge at Wormer, but that does not affect Zaanstad.

133786.explanation: That should have happened more than 30 years ago.

133787.explanation: A growing municipality needs a growing infrastructure.

133788.explanation: Lowering the railway is the best way to connect large parts of Zaanstad together and provide good accessibility.

133790.explanation: It is ridiculous that a night train between Alkmaar and Amsterdam does not stop in Zaanstad. For the NS this brings extra passengers and money, and Zaanstad certainly should not have to pay too much for that.

133791.explanation: The A8/A9 is essential for accessibility, safety and livability. PVV Zaanstad chooses to do what is necessary in the interest of our residents.

133792.explanation: Opening this bridge for car traffic between Poelenburg and the Achtersluispolder would cause a huge amount of cut-through traffic to the Thorbeckeweg.

133793.explanation: Car ownership is still increasing in Zaanstad, despite the discouragement policy pursued in recent years. Burdening residents with a parking problem is not a solution.

133794.explanation: PVV Zaanstad chooses cheaper, fair and logical parking policy. Parking is a basic provision, not a punitive measure.

133795.explanation: All plans for environmental zones must stop; they harm the economy and limit accessibility.

133796.explanation: 30 km/h in busy residential neighborhoods is of course advisable, but 30 km/h on the Provincial Road is of course ridiculous.

133797.explanation: Almost every neighborhood has a community center or district building; these can and must also facilitate youth well.

133798.explanation: Almost every neighborhood has a community center or district building; these can and must also facilitate elderly people well.

133799.explanation: Informal caregivers are indispensable. Appreciation must increase again, without Stadspas hassle. But respite care may be just as important, so that informal caregivers can care better without collapsing themselves.

133801.explanation: We find school swimming very important, but only for children who attend school in Zaanstad.

133741.explanation: Residents must have the opportunity to express their opinion on important decisions.

133742.explanation: The property tax (ozb) should not be lowered. Zaanstad is already relatively low compared to other municipalities. The revenues from the property tax are needed for good care, greenery and culture, among other things.

133743.explanation: It is important to involve residents at an early stage in the municipality’s plans.

133746.explanation: Zaanstad must give homeowners the option to redeem their leasehold. The price depends on the situation.

133747.explanation: No more business parks need to be created in Zaanstad. However, we must keep the sites that are currently available for businesses also available for businesses.

133748.explanation: Business activity is important for the Zaan economy, but this must not be at the expense of livability and health of our residents. Pollution must therefore be prevented as much as possible.

133749.explanation: It is important that green space remains available in the city alongside housing and work. If we allow industry to expand, this often comes at the expense of housing, work or nature.

133750.explanation: People who have received a residence permit must be helped to find a home just like other vulnerable groups.

133751.explanation: We find it important that when people sleep outside in tents, Zaanstad takes its share to prevent this. Also if this means that we have to shelter more asylum seekers than legally required.

133752.explanation: This can lead to undesirable effects such as dumping waste next to the containers or across municipal boundaries.

133753.explanation: We are in favor of a new innovative economy where companies work sustainably. We find it important that a clear choice is made on this, so that new companies wanting to establish themselves in Zaanstad know what to expect.

133754.explanation: To achieve the climate goals, all forms of sustainable energy generation are needed. At the same time, as much as possible should be aimed at dual use.

133756.explanation: Reaching this goal by 2040 is sufficiently ambitious.

133757.explanation: Not all houses can be disconnected from gas by this time. All new houses must, however, be built off the gas grid.

133758.explanation: To achieve the climate goals, all forms of sustainable energy generation are needed. Wind turbines make a large contribution but cannot be placed everywhere. For example, we still see possibilities along the North Sea Canal.

133759.explanation: A careful process has taken place for this location. D66 commits to the outcome of that process.

133760.explanation: Night flights cause a lot of nuisance.

133761.explanation: It is important that everyone in Zaanstad can participate in sports.

133763.explanation: Many residents of Zaanstad enjoy events and festivals. D66 believes there should be more space for these.

133764.explanation: Sports are good for the health and well-being of our residents. There should be more space for this in public areas.

133765.explanation: Sports clubs must be pleasant places to play and exercise.

133766.explanation: We believe that protecting as many historical buildings as possible as monuments contributes to identity, sustainability and livability, and at the same time prevents valuable heritage from disappearing due to short-term thinking. There must still be room for forward-looking development here.

133767.explanation: Enforcement must be deployed in the parts of the municipality where it is most needed.

133768.explanation: Camera surveillance can be important for safety, but it also infringes on people’s privacy. The current policy is sufficiently balanced.

133770.explanation: Unfortunately, there are too many incidents with stabbing weapons. Education can help.

133771.explanation: The nuisance in Zaandam East and Zaanstad North must be addressed. More funding is needed for this. According to D66 Zaanstad, this money should be invested in prevention.

133772.explanation: The housing shortage is severe, so we believe it is important to realize as many new homes as possible.

133773.explanation: There must be a balanced mix of housing.

133774.explanation: Green gardens can make an important contribution to climate adaptation, and D66 Zaanstad wants to encourage this as much as possible.

133775.explanation: By demolishing old buildings and, for example, building homes, we can create more housing in Zaanstad, which is urgently needed.

133776.explanation: For every new residential area, sufficient local facilities such as education, healthcare, shops, and sports & culture must also be considered.

133777.explanation: D66 Zaanstad believes the municipality should counteract student housing because it strains livability and social cohesion in neighborhoods. At the same time, a tailored approach is needed: address abuses but offer enough affordable housing alternatives so that shared living remains a choice, not a necessity.

133778.explanation: We believe it is important that residents of Zaanstad should be able to continue living in their own neighborhood.

133779.explanation: Disagree, building more social rental housing comes at the expense of other homes such as mid-priced rental and owner-occupied housing. We value a mix of housing.

133780.explanation: People should be free to decide what they do with their own home.

133781.explanation: Business parks are necessary for economic activity. There are other places in Zaanstad where housing can be developed.

133782.explanation: D66 does not support building on green spaces, but limited construction should be possible on the outskirts of villages. D66 opposes building in Natura 2000 areas.

133783.explanation: D66 Zaanstad believes the municipality should fight poverty, because financial stress limits opportunities and increases inequality. However, this should not be done by investing more in poverty policy but rather in prevention, education, and sports.

133784.explanation: Everyone should be able to make a meaningful contribution to society. Moreover, this promotes transition into paid work.

133785.explanation: D66 does not support the construction of additional roads and/or bridges.

133786.explanation: This promotes traffic flow and accessibility of Zaanstad West.

133787.explanation: D66 is not in favor of laying extra asphalt. Instead, investment should be made in public transport and cycling.

133788.explanation: To improve Zaanstad's accessibility by train, part of the railway should be moved underground.

133790.explanation: Many residents of Zaanstad use Amsterdam’s nightlife. It is important that they can return home safely and sustainably.

133791.explanation: D66 opposes laying extra asphalt, especially through green spaces and UNESCO world heritage sites.

133792.explanation: D66 Zaanstad believes the Butterfly Bridge should not be opened to cars, as this compromises traffic safety, livability, and sustainable mobility. The bridge is intended to safely connect cyclists and pedestrians and thus promote a low-car, healthy city.

133793.explanation: D66 wants to reduce the number of parking spaces at ground level. This does not necessarily mean fewer parking spaces overall.

133794.explanation: People should be encouraged to travel by bike, on foot, or by public transport.

133795.explanation: We are positive about expanding environmental zones because clean air is essential for health and livability. A municipality-wide environmental zone can, if introduced fairly, reduce emissions with good transitional arrangements and support for residents and businesses.

133796.explanation: This is safer and better for the environment.

133797.explanation: It is important that young people have a place to meet without causing nuisance.

133798.explanation: Care in one’s own neighborhood is important.

133799.explanation: Informal caregivers are very important and should receive recognition for it.

133800.explanation: Equal opportunities for everyone start at every child’s cradle.

133801.explanation: Disagree, as we find an income-dependent contribution more appropriate.

133741.explanation: The CDA believes that referendums are not a good way to involve residents. They oversimplify complex choices and often create more divisions. Residents are better involved through public speaking, consultations, and good participation in plans that affect them.

133742.explanation: The CDA considers a lower property tax (ozb) desirable but not realistic. Less ozb means less money for care, facilities, and maintenance in the municipality. This will negatively affect things residents need daily. Therefore, lowering it is not easily feasible.

133743.explanation: Resident participation is important because they know best what is happening in their neighborhood or village. However, clear agreements are essential on how input from neighborhood and village councils is incorporated into decisions.

133746.explanation: It is good and important to offer homeowners clarity and options regarding leasehold. A favorable buyout can help some, but there are too many pitfalls to do this carefully.

133747.explanation: It is important that Zaanstad provides space for living, exercise, recreation, green areas, and entrepreneurship. To maintain that balance, expanding business parks is undesirable. Existing sites should be well maintained and remain attractive to entrepreneurs.

133748.explanation: Companies must comply with rules, and pollution is unacceptable. At the same time, supervision should remain proportional. Enforcement where necessary, cooperation where possible, with attention to a healthy living environment and a strong local business sector.

133749.explanation: Zaanstad has industry, nature, business activity, and housing side by side. To keep the city livable, it is important that nature is preserved and has enough space.

133750.explanation: Status holders must be able to integrate and need housing, but structural priority increases pressure on social rental housing. A fair distribution is necessary, weighing both legal obligations and opportunities for other housing seekers.

133751.explanation: Zaanstad actively participates in a regional approach to reception. We take our regional responsibility and look beyond our own borders. That we sometimes accommodate more asylum seekers than legally required, we see as a positive contribution.

133752.explanation: CDA Zaanstad believes that a financial incentive does not improve the waste system. There is a high risk that residents unwilling or unable to pay will dump waste illegally, causing more litter and nuisance in public spaces.

133753.explanation: The CDA considers it important that companies become more sustainable and believes we should give them the opportunity to do so. Excluding companies in advance contradicts our business-friendly climate.

133754.explanation: Solar panels on agricultural land do not fit with the open character of Zaanstad and harm the landscape. Moreover, it has recently become clear that solar fields can cause disturbances for, among others, pilots and can therefore result in unsafe situations.

133756.explanation: The national and European goal is to be climate neutral by 2050. Zaanstad has already been ahead by 10 years. We consider that a fitting and sufficiently ambitious objective.

133757.explanation: The CDA disagrees with the statement that all houses must be free of gas by 2040. This is unrealistic and unfeasible due to the large investments and limited time. However, it is logical to build new homes without gas from the start.

133758.explanation: Wind turbines affect the view, can have health effects, and take up a lot of space. In Zaanstad, there are already enough wind turbines. Small wind turbines for farmers’ own use are appropriate, and we would welcome their expansion.

133759.explanation: It is ridiculous that as a municipality we are only allowed to advise on our own territory. The proposed high-voltage route with associated transformer stations is harmful to our open landscape, nature, the agricultural sector, and above all the health of our residents.

133760.explanation: Night flights cause nuisance and disrupt the sleep of our residents. It was previously promised that the nuisance would decrease, but it is time to really put an end to night flights. Zaanstad must continue to put pressure on the national government.

133761.explanation: Sports clubs are important for health, meeting people, and connection in our society. As a municipality, we bear responsibility for a healthy and cohesive community and therefore support clubs facing difficulties.

133763.explanation: We believe there certainly must be space for events, but this does not necessarily require a permanent location. Changing locations can have charm and multifunctional use, keeping them attractive and flexible.

133764.explanation: Exercising outdoors is healthy and contributes to vitality and social interaction. Zaanstad already has places for this, but more facilities make sports accessible to everyone and stimulate an active, engaged community.

133765.explanation: Good clubhouses and fields make sports clubs more attractive and strengthen society. They provide space for sports and other activities, thereby creating added value for clubs and residents alike.

133766.explanation: The goal is to preserve the historic character of the Zaan region. Monument status is not always necessary; important is historical awareness and careful consideration per building for proper preservation and responsible maintenance.

133767.explanation: There is a shortage of enforcement officers and police on the streets. Safety is under pressure; therefore, more enforcement officers and police are needed to ensure public safety in all its forms and to make residents feel safe.

133768.explanation: Camera surveillance can contribute to a safer municipality, alongside prevention, more police presence, and better lighting. It is one of the ways to enhance safety and make residents feel more secure.

133770.explanation: The municipality already provides education about the dangers of knives, which is important and effective. Prevention and good education require perseverance but work in the long term and therefore remain essential.

133771.explanation: Youth nuisance is growing in Zaandam East and Zaanstad North. Youth are our future; therefore, we invest in guidance and opportunities. This strengthens a safe, connected society and helps young people stay on the right path.

133772.explanation: The demand for sufficient and affordable housing is high: for elderly people who want to move and starters who need to enter the housing market. Adding homes is essential to give everyone in Krommenie and Wormerveer a place to feel at home.

133773.explanation: In new construction, a mix of cheap, mid-range, and social housing is essential. This way we serve all housing target groups and strengthen the connection between neighborhoods and villages so everyone finds a place they feel at home.

133774.explanation: There is already a free tile collection service for greening gardens. No additional rewards are needed; residents must see the value of greenery in their garden themselves and experience its importance for the environment and living conditions.

133775.explanation: Sometimes it is necessary to replace old or poorly maintained buildings with new construction. The CDA considers it important that new buildings fit the environment and contribute to an attractive and livable neighborhood.

133776.explanation: For a livable neighborhood, basic facilities nearby are essential: school, general practitioner, childcare, and, in our view, also a sports club. This helps residents feel at home quickly and keeps the neighborhood lively, connected, and attractive for everyone.

133777.explanation: Dividing homes into multiple units causes nuisance and poor living conditions. The CDA will continue to actively address this to improve livability in neighborhoods and ensure that homes are used fairly and responsibly.

133778.explanation: Social rental homes are primarily for Zaanstad residents who want to continue living here. The CDA believes they should be given priority in allocation so people can stay in their own neighborhood and the community is strengthened.

133779.explanation: We consider the current percentage of 30% social rental housing appropriate. There must remain space for a mix of homes to serve all target groups and keep Zaanstad livable and attractive for everyone.

133780.explanation: A self-occupancy obligation strengthens livability and ensures a level playing field in the housing market. It discourages investors and, for example, makes it easier for starters to buy a home because there is less competition from investors.

133781.explanation: Living on industrial sites is not obvious and can limit residential enjoyment due to business activities. Where possible, this can be explored, but the attractiveness and functionality of the sites remain leading.

133782.explanation: At the edges of villages there is often unused space suitable for small-scale development. Because space is limited, building must be handled creatively without fully covering green areas, so livability and nature are preserved.

133783.explanation: Zaanstad already invests heavily in combating poverty. More important than more money is that spending remains effective so residents are truly helped and support is tailored to their situation.

133784.explanation: Not everyone with social assistance benefits is able to perform mandatory community work or follow education. Where possible this is desirable, but customization is needed so support remains appropriate and feasible for everyone.

133785.explanation: At this moment, we see no use or necessity for an additional bridge over the Zaan. More important is that existing connections and access roads are well maintained and improved where needed, so traffic flow and accessibility remain optimal.

133786.explanation: The bus bridge De Binding is already open 24 hours a day, which we welcome. This full connection strengthens the link between Westerwatering and the rest of Zaanstad and makes travel faster, safer, and easier for residents.

133787.explanation: Good access roads are needed for new homes. Additional roads help prevent congestion and ensure smooth traffic flow, tackling through traffic in villages so accessibility and livability go hand in hand.

133788.explanation: To increase train frequency, the Zaan line must be widened. Going underground can prevent congestion at crossings and improve the living environment, provided sufficient resources exist and cooperation among all parties runs smoothly.

133790.explanation: There is little demand for a night train between Zaanstad and Amsterdam. A night bus already runs, and the CDA prefers optimizing this existing connection rather than investing in a night train.

133791.explanation: It is unacceptable that Zaanse villages absorb regional traffic pressure. The A8/A9 connection is crucial for the safety and health of residents. Therefore, Zaanstad will continue to urge the national government and province to construct this connection.

133792.explanation: Cars over the Vlinderbrug cause extra through traffic, which reduces safety and residential enjoyment. Therefore, opening it up is unwise; Poelenburg must remain a safe and pleasant residential area for residents.

133793.explanation: Many neighborhoods already have a shortage of parking spaces. Fewer spaces cause more nuisance due to illegal parking. Careful consideration of layout is needed in new construction to maintain sufficient parking space.

133794.explanation: Paid parking is necessary to regulate parking pressure and distribute space fairly. Making parking cheaper does not solve problems. However, parking policy must be clear, explainable, and consistently applied.

133795.explanation: The intention is to first introduce three small environmental zones in Zaanstad. The CDA has actually advocated postponing this, among other reasons for smaller entrepreneurs. A full municipal environmental zone is excessive, unrealistic, and requires more support.

133796.explanation: Lower speeds in built-up areas help improve traffic safety. By reducing car speeds, streets become safer for residents, cyclists, and children in our villages and city.

133797.explanation: If young people need a meeting place, it should be provided. This could be a sports club or something else. It doesn't always have to be arranged by the municipality; it is more effective when ideas come from the youth and the municipality facilitates.

133798.explanation: Good care and social contact for the elderly are important, but a care neighborhood center in every village is not necessarily mandatory. Needs differ per village.
