Where needed, appropriate, accessible support should be provided, preferably nearby and in cooperation with existing facilities.

133799.explanation: Informal caregivers are priceless, but appreciation is not about money, but about time and attention. The CDA therefore focuses on respite care, so that informal caregivers get a break and a much-needed breathing space.

133800.explanation: Free pregnancy courses are often already offered through the Public Health Service (GGD). The CDA finds it important that (expectant) parents are properly referred to the GGD and receive additional courses and information for optimal support.

133801.explanation: Making swimming lessons completely free is not a municipal responsibility and puts pressure on swimming pools. Everyone should be able to take swimming lessons; for those who cannot afford it, there are schemes. More important is the reintroduction of school swimming for all children to encourage swimming.

133741.explanation: Residents must be able to participate in decision-making about plans that have major consequences.

133742.explanation: Housing is already expensive enough for many people.

133743.explanation: Residents know what is happening in their neighborhood or village.

133746.explanation: This gives residents more security about their housing.

133747.explanation: There is little space in the city. Outside the city is needed for jobs.

133748.explanation: The health of residents comes first.

133749.explanation: Sometimes necessary for employment, with care for nature and the environment.

133751.explanation: Zaanstad helps people, but pays attention to affordability and livability.

133752.explanation: This causes more litter on the streets.

133753.explanation: Companies must deal responsibly with people and the environment.

133756.explanation: The municipality should entice, not oblige. Residents determine the pace.

133757.explanation: Only if it remains affordable. No extra obligations for those who already contribute enough.

133760.explanation: People have a right to peace and sleep.

133761.explanation: Sports connect people and promote health.

133767.explanation: Visible enforcement provides a feeling of safety.

133768.explanation: Where necessary for safety, as a tailored approach.

133773.explanation: Distribution differs per neighborhood. Residents are involved in the discussion.

133774.explanation: Encouraging is allowed, obliging is not.

133775.explanation: New construction is often more sustainable and healthier.

133776.explanation: Also helps against abuse and slumlords.

133778.explanation: Many young people want to continue living in Zaanstad.

133780.explanation: Houses are meant to live in.

133781.explanation: This can be combined with working and reduces traffic.

133782.explanation: Necessary due to housing pressure, while preserving green spaces.

133783.explanation: No one should be left out.

133784.explanation: Encouragement is allowed, but no coercion.

133787.explanation: Cars remain necessary for many people.

133788.explanation: That makes neighborhoods calmer and safer.

133790.explanation: Only if necessary and used well.

133792.explanation: Wish of the neighborhood and better for traffic and safety.

133793.explanation: More parking spaces are actually needed.

133795.explanation: This hits people with little money too hard.

133797.explanation: Young people need their own place.

133800.explanation: A good start for parents and child.

133741.explanation: We make plans with residents, through citizen assemblies and local participation. That leads to better-informed choices. For major issues, a well-drafted referendum can help find positive, concrete solutions.

133742.explanation: For a healthy living environment, good healthcare (including youth care), and more housing, extra funds are needed in the coming years. Zaanstad faces major financial challenges due to national budget cuts. Lower property taxes mean other important services can no longer be provided.

133743.explanation: Participation must be overhauled: besides neighborhood councils, we also want to facilitate neighborhood referenda. Additionally, we want citizen assemblies on major themes. By facilitating neighborhood budgets and a neighborhood budget plan, residents gain the opportunity to make their neighborhood livable for people and animals.

133746.explanation: On land owned by Zaanstad, the municipality can maintain control. Therefore, the Party for the Animals generally does not want ground leases to be bought out, but wants them to be low for households so people don’t get into financial trouble.

133747.explanation: In scarce space, we must make choices. More green outdoor spaces and nature are needed, more housing, and more facilities like community centers. The Party for the Animals prefers to focus on this rather than more industrial estates and more possessions. We optimally accommodate sustainable companies in existing industrial estates.

133748.explanation: Environmental enforcement becomes a high priority at the environmental service, because a clean living environment is essential for our health and nature. Companies that violate rules will be fined or closed more quickly.

133749.explanation: Many animal and plant species are going extinct because we allow nature to be polluted and fragmented. Severely endangered species live here in Zaanstad as well. That is why we must do much more to protect, restore, and expand nature.

133750.explanation: Status holders are prioritized because, like others in need, they urgently require housing. The real problem is the shortage: housing corporations have been stripped by previous national governments, while demand for social rental housing grew. Zaanstad must support corporations to build significantly more.

133751.explanation: Due to the national shortage of shelters, asylum seekers sleep outside and procedures take too long. Therefore, every municipality must do what it can. Zaanstad has committed to doing more than the standard. We must continue to urge other municipalities to at least fulfill their obligations.

133752.explanation: People and businesses that produce more waste must pay more for it. We want to reward waste separation so that more can be recycled. Further research should determine if a pay-per-use system (diftar) works best.

133753.explanation: Zaanstad must steer which companies settle here. We can allocate space specifically for sustainable, social, and future-proof businesses. Support for local circular start-ups ensures more local employment and social cohesion.

133754.explanation: Agricultural land is not meant for solar farms, but for plant-based food production and nature development, and if livestock farming shrinks significantly, for housing. Solar panels and greenery will become standard on roofs, facades, sound barriers, and above parking lots.

133756.explanation: Due to government negligence, climate goals are at risk, while time is running out and the effects are already visible locally. Therefore, Zaanstad must become climate neutral as soon as possible, aiming for 2030.

133757.explanation: To be climate neutral by 2030, Zaanstad must quickly phase out gas for cooking and heating. This must remain affordable and use truly sustainable heat sources. The municipality ensures supervision and targeted subsidies. For monuments, we will explore alternatives such as hydrogen.

133758.explanation: Zaanstad stimulates wind energy in locations where people, animals, and nature experience little or no disturbance, for example in the North Sea Canal port and industrial sites. No wind turbines will be placed in nature areas or areas with vulnerable bird or bat populations.

133759.explanation: To ensure sustainable energy supply, the energy network must be significantly strengthened. We are therefore not inherently against expanding the network, but the impact on people, animals, and nature must be minimal. Sustainable energy must not go to unnecessary business activities.

133760.explanation: People get sick from Schiphol. Residents nearby face daily nuisance, leading to mental and physical complaints. The government should protect its citizens against this. Therefore, the Party for the Animals wants a direct stop to night flights and a significant reduction of Schiphol.

133761.explanation: Due to mergers and fewer sports facilities, sports are less accessible. Zaanstad must help associations more often to stay afloat. Where possible, link support to goals such as sports for children from poor families and people with disabilities, and making club buildings sustainable.

133763.explanation: The Omweg event site in Assendelft is suitable as a permanent location: the impact on residents and nature is smallest here. Therefore, we will critically examine the consequences for people, animals, and nature at any potential alternative locations.

133764.explanation: The municipality designs public spaces so everyone can safely play sports and exercise, including through associations. Sport must be accessible and inclusive, with facilities in every neighborhood: from exercise gardens and fitness spots to fields and skate parks.

133765.explanation: Zaanstad must invest in sports on real grass and replace artificial turf with rubber granulate as soon as possible. If necessary, we will also allocate budget for making clubhouses more sustainable and for investments that make sports more accessible.

133766.explanation: Protecting heritage is important for our Zaanstad history and culture. But the municipality is changing: there must also be room for sustainability and new forms of housing and facilities. Monument status limits flexibility, so we must handle it carefully and not want to designate everything.

133767.explanation: More enforcement capacity is needed for environmental crimes, animal abuse, illegal tree felling, street intimidation and discrimination, and for protecting peaceful demonstrations. By investing in the social domain (including night shelters, good care), community enforcement officers (BOAs) gain more time for these tasks.

133768.explanation: Privacy concerns lead to very cautious use of camera surveillance in public spaces. Camera surveillance offers only a false sense of security and often only causes the problem to be displaced. Automatic facial recognition is too intrusive and will not be used.

133770.explanation: Gun ownership must be drastically reduced. Education is necessary but not sufficient. Poverty and racism can play a role: young people sometimes feel they have nothing left to lose. Focus on connecting with youth and organize places where they can meet. Invest in youth workers.

133771.explanation: Through PACT Zaandam East we invest in guidance for young people, support for children in poverty, and tackling nuisance and crime. Extra attention is also given to youth nuisance in North. We prefer to use extra resources on prevention and talent development for lasting better results.

133772.explanation: Zaanstad can build beautiful, green neighborhoods within the city. In Zaanstad-North (including Assendelft) facilities and public transport must grow accordingly. Ecological routes are a prerequisite; projects that do not meet this will not proceed.

133773.explanation: The social rental housing stock has shrunk while many Zaanstad residents and young people wait too long for affordable housing. The solution is building more: more social rental homes to shorten waiting times. Merely focusing on move-ups to owner-occupied and private rental sectors is insufficient.

133774.explanation: Green gardens provide space for animals and insects, offer shade, and retain rainwater. Therefore, Zaanstad continues to remove paving stones and stimulates greening through education and subsidies, such as facade greenery, small front gardens, native plants, rain barrels, and green roofs.

133775.explanation: Renovation and redevelopment take precedence over demolition and new construction: it is more sustainable and protects animals living in and around older buildings. Characteristic buildings, trees, avenues, and neighborhoods are preserved.

133776.explanation: When adding housing, social facilities such as schools, green spaces, care centers, public transport, parks, playgrounds, and shops are always included. Zaanstad maximizes the use of multiple purposes for space.

133777.explanation: Housing sharing or splitting can help with the housing shortage. However, it must fit within the neighborhood and not come at the expense of public space and greenery. We set clear rules to prevent abuses.

133778.explanation: More priority for local housing seekers sounds logical but can backfire: if other municipalities (such as Amsterdam) do the same, the freedom of choice for Zaankanters shrinks. Therefore, we advocate a balanced priority policy and more social rental construction in the region.

133779.explanation: There will be more affordable homes: at least 40% of rental homes will be social housing. This should finally lead to growth in the number of social rental homes in Zaanstad in the coming years.

133780.explanation: Measures against speculation with housing and vacancy will be introduced, such as an owner-occupancy obligation and a vacancy ordinance with sanctions. Houses are meant for living, not for profit.

133781.explanation: A healthy living environment is one where noise, odor, light, and air quality do not hinder you - both day and night. This cannot be guaranteed on many industrial estates in Zaanstad. Therefore, we basically do not build homes on industrial sites.

133782.explanation: Housing is a basic right. We choose real solutions: tackling vacancy and making properties suitable for living; densifying where necessary with facilities that grow along and nature-inclusive building; and converting farmland used for livestock farming into nature, with part designated for housing.

133783.explanation: Poverty results from growing inequality and is reinforced by discrimination. We want a livable income, affordable rent, and groceries. With a basic income pilot, generous social assistance, more shelters for homeless people, and simple poverty schemes without nonsensical checks.

133784.explanation: The Participation Act has failed. We see that coercion into paid work does not work. Every social assistance recipient has their own story; the municipality must listen and take this into account. Offering education can help, but we must also value volunteer work and informal care.

133785.explanation: If there is a need, for example for improved public transport/bicycle connections or nature development (ecological connections), this can be investigated.

133786.explanation: Opening the Bus Bridge during rush hour makes Westerkoog dangerously busy, costs many old trees due to ring road adjustments, and increases nitrogen in the Westzijderveld. That does not aid nature restoration. We should discourage car use rather than facilitate it more.

133787.explanation: Polluting traffic is discouraged, clean traffic is encouraged. This significantly contributes to climate goals, creates a healthier and more attractive municipality, reclaims space for greenery in our streets, and makes it safer for children and animals.

133788.explanation: Certainly! By putting the Zaanlijn underground, more trains can run. Additionally, various neighborhoods will be directly connected again, creating space for parks and extra housing. Noise nuisance from trains through Zaanstad will also decrease. Many benefits for everyone!

133790.explanation: A night train to Zaanstad can improve both the feeling of safety and convenience for travelers. Zaanstad should subsidize the NS to stop at at least two Zaanstad stations during the night as well.

133791.explanation: The A8/A9 connection shifts the problem and sends more cars through Koog aan de Zaan. For cleaner air in Zaanstad-North, fewer polluting cars should be driven. Instead of an expensive road through Natura 2000/world heritage, we opt for an environmental zone.

133792.explanation: This bridge is intended for public transport. Eventually, the quality of high-quality public transport through Zaandam depends on a connection with Amsterdam. Opening the Vlinderbrug to cars causes traffic jams and lowers public transport quality. We should not want that.

133793.explanation: We want a livable municipality, with sufficient greenery, space for animals, and play areas for children. Where there are enough parking spaces, some can be converted into small nature or playgrounds. We also want to make paved parking spaces greener, for example with semi-permeable tiles.

133794.explanation: Parking takes up a large part of public space, which is also needed for greenery, animals, play, and social meetings. By encouraging other forms of transport, parking garages, and park-and-ride facilities, the number of parking spaces can be reduced. Parking fees should contribute to this.

133795.explanation: Of course! We must get rid of fossil fuel transport. By making the whole of Zaanstad an environmental zone, we take a clear step towards a healthy and livable future. The municipality should further support people to help them make the quick switch to fossil-free transportation.

133796.explanation: A lower speed reduces the chance of accidents. It also creates a more pleasant environment with less traffic noise. Cycling, walking, and public transport become more attractive. With the space freed up, we tackle heat stress and water nuisance with trees and shrubs.

133797.explanation: Health is not only about physical but also mental health. For safe and healthy development, meeting others, discovering talents, exercising, and relaxing are very important for young people. A meeting place that connects with their world can contribute to this.

133798.explanation: In living arrangements such as a care neighborhood house, people can help each other and receive help from professionals. They can also share spaces and items, which is good for the environment, climate, and finances. Moreover, it reduces loneliness and strengthens social cohesion.

133799.explanation: Informal care can be a pleasant form of (extra) care for, for example, family members, but it is often also a heavy burden. Volunteer work is work too and should be rewarded. In the current system, this boils down to an 'appreciation.' €50 is far too little; €140 is more in the right direction.

133800.explanation: A good start is essential for children to develop well. A pregnancy course can contribute to that. If expectant parents cannot afford it, Zaanstad should offer it free of charge.

133801.explanation: It is important that all children learn to swim. If parents cannot afford swimming lessons, Zaanstad must ensure that these children can earn their swimming diploma for free.

133741.explanation: The SP is even in favor of replacing the advisory referendum with a binding corrective referendum, which allows local politicians to be overruled.

133742.explanation: The SP has no other opinion on the property tax (ozb) than that it should not be too high or too low.

133743.explanation: The SP wants democracy back in the neighborhoods. Therefore, neighborhood councils should be reintroduced, where residents genuinely have say over their own neighborhood.

133746.explanation: The SP believes that the implementation rules, with annual parameters, must be followed for converting leasehold land.

133747.explanation: The SP believes more industrial estates increase traffic pressure, and the green open space around the city can no longer bear this pressure. Zaanstad should change and grow within the built-up area.

133748.explanation: There are still factories in Zaanstad that do not meet current environmental standards. Moreover, even more industry does not meet future environmental standards. Industry must comply with current and future environmental standards in the short term. Strict enforcement must be part of this.

133749.explanation: There are no fewer than three European nature reserves around Zaanstad. In these three nature reserves, nature is strictly protected. We must take this into account in Zaanstad. Nature must also be protected at the edges of these reserves. Industry does not belong there.

133750.explanation: In 2023, 6.9% of the available social housing in the Zaan region went to status holders. That is not much. Zaanstad can therefore continue to give priority for social housing, also to status holders.

133751.explanation: Asylum seekers accommodated in Zaanstad are not only for Zaanstad itself, but are part of the regional distribution. No disturbances have been reported. There is no reason why Zaanstad should not accommodate more asylum seekers than legally required.

133752.explanation: Paying per use for underground containers (DIFTAR) carries a high risk of illegal dumping and additional waste being placed, because people do not want to pay for their waste. Moreover, it is unfair to large families and people with a lot of medical waste. They have no choice.

133753.explanation: The SP believes industry must become much cleaner and comply in the short term with current and future environmental requirements. We cannot afford new polluters. It is therefore logical that only companies operating sustainably may establish themselves in our municipality.

133754.explanation: The SP believes that as many roofs as possible should be covered with solar panels. This applies to the roofs of homes as well as commercial buildings. Agricultural land should be used for sustainable, non-polluting farming or for new nature.

133756.explanation: The SP believes that Zaanstad should be climate neutral by 2040. Even better if the goal is achieved earlier. The municipality must make efforts to achieve this.

133757.explanation: New homes may no longer be connected to the gas network, and existing homes must be taken off gas. The SP wants this to happen without residents facing higher costs. Affordability for residents is important to the SP.

133758.explanation: The energy transition requires both solar and wind energy. Wind turbines cannot be placed everywhere; there are valuable nature reserves in and around Zaanstad. If wind turbines are not placed in nature areas and cause no nuisance to residents, more wind turbines may be installed in Zaanstad.

133759.explanation: The SP believes that there should be no high-voltage pylons in the Assendelft and Westzaan polders.

133760.explanation: Night flights to and from Schiphol must end. More strongly, the SP believes Schiphol’s growth should be stopped and efforts made to reduce the number of flights to and from Schiphol significantly below current levels.

133761.explanation: The SP believes that sports must be accessible and affordable for everyone. Also for people with low incomes, children, and the elderly. The SP supports municipal financial contributions that enable this.

133763.explanation: If spatially feasible, there is no nuisance for residents, and nature is spared, an event terrain can be established in Zaanstad North.

133764.explanation: The SP believes there should be more exercise equipment for young and old in public spaces.

133765.explanation: The SP believes that sports must be accessible and affordable for everyone. Also for people with low incomes, children, and the elderly. The SP supports municipal financial contributions to enable this. Naturally, the maintenance condition of fields and clubhouses must not be poor.

133766.explanation: When the municipality grants historical buildings monument status, it helps ensure that these buildings are preserved.

133767.explanation: The SP believes there are still too few police officers and supervisors in Zaanstad and wants to invest in more police officers and even more supervisors. The role of this additional ‘blue brigade’ should focus more on preventing criminal and undesirable behavior than on punishing it.

133768.explanation: There is already a lot of camera surveillance in the municipality. Camera surveillance must be effective and not provide only a false sense of security. Moreover, the SP is concerned about the infringement of residents’ privacy due to (too many) cameras in public spaces.

133770.explanation: The municipality of Zaanstad already provides education to young people about the dangers of possessing and using stabbing weapons. Additionally, parents, neighbors, coaches, and teachers are asked to discuss the topic and encourage Zaanstad’s youth to give up weapons.

133771.explanation: The municipality is already investing additional resources to tackle nuisance in Saendelft. It also invests extra in cooperation to address youth problems in Zaanstad North and Zaandam East. For the SP, this is sufficient. Nuisance should be addressed when incidents occur.

133772.explanation: The housing shortage in Zaanstad is huge. Therefore, 5,000 homes must be built in Krommenie and Wormerveer according to plan.

133773.explanation: New construction should focus on building social rental housing. The housing shortage is greatest for people entitled to social rental housing.

133774.explanation: The SP believes residents may be asked not to fully pave their gardens but to plant at least two-thirds with flowers, shrubs, and trees. If the municipality offers a reward, this will help.

133775.explanation: Renovating and making buildings sustainable should be the norm, but sometimes demolition and new construction is a better solution.

133776.explanation: The livability of new neighborhoods suffers if residents must wait years for facilities such as public transport, a school, a supermarket, childcare, and a general practitioner.

133777.explanation: The SP is against subdividing homes into multiple rooms (verkamering), but wants to encourage friends living together, shared housing, splitting homes, and lodging rental (if well coordinated and controlled). This helps combat the housing shortage and homelessness.

133778.explanation: The SP has a caveat here. It is already possible to allocate 50% of social rental homes to applicants from the own municipality. In 2024, 73% of available social rental homes were rented to Zaanstad residents. Local residents already have priority.

133779.explanation: There is a housing shortage in Zaanstad. The greatest shortage is among people entitled to social rental housing. At least 40% of new homes must therefore be social rental housing. The SP believes even a higher percentage of social rental housing should be built.

133780.explanation: The municipality must ensure that the owner-occupancy obligation is expanded. This prevents homes from being bought by investors who do not intend to live in them themselves.

133781.explanation: Homes will soon be built on the Achtersluispolder industrial estate in Zaanstad. Where possible, the SP believes homes may also be built on other industrial estates.

133782.explanation: To protect and preserve nature and green areas in and around Zaanstad, the municipality should limit building to the cores rather than the edges of these areas.

133783.explanation: Many children (and their parents) in Zaanstad still live in poverty. Poverty has far-reaching consequences for people and society, such as health problems, limited access to education, and social exclusion. The municipality must invest more money in combating poverty.

133784.explanation: For people receiving social assistance benefits, the focus should be on what someone wants and can do, rather than what they must or may do. Give social assistance recipients space so that through activities of their own choice, they can continue participating in society.

133785.explanation: It depends where the additional bridge should be built. Currently, the municipalities of Zaanstad and Wormerland are investigating a third bridge over the Zaan between Wormerveer and Wormer. The SP awaits the research results.

133786.explanation: The SP opposes 24-hour opening of the Bus Bridge De Binding because it harms the Natura 2000 area Polder Westzaan and because much more (car) traffic will come to the Westerkoog ring road. This ring road would have to be rebuilt and trees cut down.

133787.explanation: The SP believes the municipality should prioritize building and improving bicycle connections and public transport. Furthermore, the SP believes no new (car) roads should be constructed in or near Natura 2000 areas. The A8-A9 motorway must be permanently cancelled, also through Zaanstad.

133788.explanation: Undergrounding the Zaanlijn offers prospects. More homes can be built, and the connection between neighborhoods in the east and west of Zaanstad will improve.

133790.explanation: There is an excellent night bus connection between Amsterdam and Krommenie-Assendelft. Buses run every hour at night from both Amsterdam and Krommenie. The night bus also stops at all stations in Zaanstad and has many more stops along the route.

133791.explanation: The A8/A9 connecting road will not be built. This is due to environmental, heritage, traffic congestion elsewhere, and the large amount of money involved. Zaanstad should stop pushing the national and provincial governments and accept that the road cannot be built.

133792.explanation: Bus Bridge De Vlinder is intended to ensure smooth flow of buses. If De Vlinder is opened to cars, public transport accessibility will deteriorate. Therefore, this bus bridge should not be opened to cars.

133793.explanation: The SP wants to use a well-thought-out parking policy to ensure that the number of cars in Zaanstad's neighborhoods decreases. The fewer parking spaces there are, the more homes can be built.

133794.explanation: Paid parking in Zaanstad can be made cheaper, provided that parking nuisance in certain streets and neighborhoods does not increase. If lowering the parking fee increases nuisance, paid parking should not be made cheaper.

133795.explanation: The entire municipality of Zaanstad can become an environmental zone, provided a buy-back scheme for old diesel vehicles is introduced.

133796.explanation: The maximum speed within built-up areas should be reduced to 30 km/h on almost all roads. 30 km zones increase traffic safety, reduce noise pollution, and contribute to healthier air quality, significantly improving the living environment. Moreover, travel time hardly increases.

133797.explanation: The SP believes that each neighborhood should have sufficient facilities for young people, including meeting places. In addition, every neighborhood should also have enough playgrounds for children.

133798.explanation: The SP believes that every neighborhood should have a care community center for the elderly. A care community center is a small-scale care facility where older people can live and age in their own neighborhood or village. Independence and community spirit are central in such a center.

133799.explanation: Informal caregivers primarily provide care to those in need from the heart. They also save the municipality money. The appreciation for informal caregivers, which makes them feel recognized, can be higher than it currently is.

133800.explanation: GGD Zaanstreek-Waterland already offers pregnancy and childbirth courses. Everyone expecting a child should be able to participate. If the course is free, every pregnant woman can join regardless of income.

133801.explanation: Swimming lessons are expensive. An increasing number of Dutch children do not have a swimming diploma. Children stop taking lessons because their parents cannot or do not want to pay the fees. Since being able to swim is important in the water-rich Zaanstreek, swimming lessons should be free for every child.

133741.explanation: A (broad) survey is a better replacement in cases where the municipality wants to involve the opinions of residents.

133742.explanation: The municipality strives to lower taxes and fees for its residents where possible. We aim to let taxes such as property tax (ozb) increase only in line with inflation.

133743.explanation: The ChristenUnie wants to encourage residents (both adults and children) to contribute ideas via platforms and other organizations prior to policy formation.

133746.explanation: Leasehold can cause uncertainty and rising costs for homeowners. By giving residents the option to voluntarily redeem their leasehold under reasonable conditions, the municipality increases housing security and control over their own home. This requires clear and transparent rules.

133747.explanation: Business activity in Zaanstad is important. However, there must always be enough space left to build homes.

133748.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes we must treat our planet with care, which is why polluting companies must be more strictly monitored.

133749.explanation: Industry is important, but should never come at the expense of nature.

133750.explanation: We strive to help status holders assigned to the municipality of Zaanstad obtain housing within a foreseeable time, preferably within six months.

133751.explanation: We are a social municipality and this is our strength. Companies are very happy with employees coming from shelter locations. We must continue discussions at local and national levels about a fair distribution.

133752.explanation: The ChristenUnie supports the current waste policy with separate waste collection and the centralized (within neighborhoods) disposal of residual waste. There must remain ongoing attention for larger families, the elderly, people with disabilities, and residents of high-rise buildings.

133753.explanation: The ChristenUnie wants as many sustainable companies as possible to establish themselves in the municipality.

133754.explanation: We also find it important that opportunities to generate solar energy on roofs and facades are utilized even more than at present.

133756.explanation: It is important to do everything within our power to achieve these goals as quickly as possible, and if it's possible to reach them sooner, we should do so.

133757.explanation: The transition to gas-free living is necessary, but the pace must be feasible and affordable. The municipality must work towards less gas use and sustainable alternatives, but cannot guarantee that all houses will be gas-free by 2040. This varies by neighborhood, housing type, and residents' situation.

133758.explanation: ChristenUnie supports wind turbines where possible, provided good prior participation with local residents takes place.

133759.explanation: It is insufficiently proven that this is truly the best location. Although we support the energy transition, a better investigation is needed to see if there are indeed no other options.

133760.explanation: Many people in Zaanstad suffer from flights passing by at night. For the health of our residents, it is important that night flights are cancelled.

133761.explanation: It is important for the city that sports clubs establish themselves and continue to exist. Therefore, it is good that municipalities financially support sports clubs.

133763.explanation: Culture is important to the ChristenUnie, but we do not want residents of Zaanstad North to experience nuisance from this permanent event site.

133764.explanation: Facilities such as a skate park, small neighborhood sports field, playground, or fitness trail deserve ongoing attention.

133765.explanation: Being active is very important, and therefore the ChristenUnie supports allocating more funds to sports clubs.

133766.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes everything must be done to prevent demolition or neglect of special buildings.

133767.explanation: Safety in the city is very important. More and more people feel unsafe; therefore, it is important that community enforcement officers (boa's) can contribute to city safety.

133768.explanation: Camera surveillance is important, but the ChristenUnie would rather spend this money on prevention.

133770.explanation: Better cooperation, education, and a strong approach are keys to reducing weapon carrying among youth.

133771.explanation: The ChristenUnie finds it important that this money is allocated to prevention and more hangout spaces for these young people, so they have a place to go other than the street.

133772.explanation: Many people who live in Krommenie and Wormerveer feel at home there. Therefore, it is good that houses are being added there for future generations.

133773.explanation: Currently, there is insufficient housing turnover, so the ChristenUnie wants emphasis also placed on medium-priced rental and owner-occupied homes.

133774.explanation: Rewarding residents who make their gardens greener can be a good incentive. A green garden helps against heat stress and water nuisance.

133775.explanation: This very much depends on what is being demolished; if it concerns heritage, then it should not be demolished.

133776.explanation: The ChristenUnie finds it important that facilities remain adequate. Many buildings are going up in Zaanstad, but the necessary facilities are often forgotten.

133777.explanation: Due to the severe housing shortage, it is important that we find living space in every possible way. By creating shared housing, starters can still get a suitable home. It is important to assess per neighborhood whether this is possible and desirable.

133778.explanation: It is important that if you live in Zaanstad and want to continue living there, you are given priority over people from outside Zaanstad. This helps residents maintain their connection to the city as well as to friends and family who often live there too.

133779.explanation: For social rental housing, flow-through is also very important; that's why the Christian Union considers it very important that affordable owner-occupied homes are built.

133780.explanation: Homes are meant to be lived in, not used for speculation. By requiring buyers to live in their own homes, the housing market remains fairer and more accessible for starters and families. This prevents properties from being bought up by investors while residents cannot find affordable housing.

133781.explanation: A business park is designed for businesses. If we start building homes there as well, residents may experience nuisance from the businesses.

133782.explanation: At the moment, we want to prioritize building within the city as much as possible, but we also recognize the need for construction elsewhere, so we might consider that in the future.

133783.explanation: The Christian Union believes the municipality should invest more in fighting poverty because no one should be left behind. We must take responsibility to help the vulnerable in society and offer them perspective.

133784.explanation: By having people receiving social assistance perform socially meaningful work, you keep them engaged in society. This must always be tailored to individual circumstances.

133785.explanation: At this time, the Christian Union sees no benefit to an additional bridge over the Zaan. Currently, we can better spend that money on other projects.

133786.explanation: The Christian Union stands for a reliable government; after multiple broken promises to Westerwatering, it is important that they fulfill their promises with actions.

133787.explanation: Road construction must have a purpose; if many homes are built in a certain neighborhood, we see advantages in building roads.

133788.explanation: The Christian Union has repeatedly advocated for a lowered railway in the Guisweg project. We believe this is the solution to the current traffic bottlenecks caused by the Zaan line.

133790.explanation: The Christian Union strives for high-quality public transport, which should also include a night train.

133791.explanation: The Christian Union believes an alternative to the A8-A9 must be developed. This should do justice to the interests of inter-local traffic and the local residents.

133792.explanation: The Vlinderbrug is a bridge specially designed for buses. Moreover, the Achtersluispolder is not set up to handle heavy car traffic.

133793.explanation: Parking pressure puts the livability of neighborhoods under strain; therefore, the Zaan parking policy should be regularly updated. Good coordination with the neighborhood is also important since some areas need more than others.

133794.explanation: The Christian Union believes parking policy is a means, not an end. Our starting point is that Zaanstad is a welcoming municipality and attractive to shoppers.

133795.explanation: The Christian Union is in favor of environmental zones, but we do not believe the entire municipality should become an environmental zone.

133796.explanation: The 30 km/h zones must be implemented, as this creates a safer street environment for cyclists and pedestrians.

133797.explanation: Meeting places for young people are very important. A place where youth feel at home and can meet each other is important in every village of Zaanstad.

133798.explanation: The Christian Union finds it important that there are places throughout Zaanstad where elderly people can go for social interaction, support, and appropriate help. This reduces loneliness.

133799.explanation: Caregivers should receive more appreciation; they are not volunteers and do their work with love and dedication. They should receive help and support from the municipality and be able to find it easily.

133800.explanation: Every parent should have the opportunity to attend a free parenting or pregnancy course.

133801.explanation: In a waterland like the Netherlands, it is important that every child learns to swim. Therefore, the Christian Union wants swimming lessons to be free.

133741.explanation: Our residents vote for parties with a wide range of positions. In our divided council, many parties are needed to form a coalition. The majority of council members do not always represent what the majority of our residents want. A referendum is a fine democratic addition.

133742.explanation: The property tax in Zaanstad is high compared to what our residents get in return. The costs should be proportional to what the municipality provides and should not increase beyond inflation.

133743.explanation: Our municipality is a collection of villages and urban areas with different neighborhoods. For Lokaal Zaans, it is very important that residents can participate in municipal plans through neighborhood and village councils.

133746.explanation: The principle is not 'cheap buyout,' but a buyout arrangement that respects the conditions under which our residents entered a leasehold construction. The arrangement must be fair and equitable, in good consultation with the Zaanstad Leasehold Interest Association.

133747.explanation: The starting point is not more business parks, but sufficient space for our companies. Currently, companies lack space, so we agree on this. Our companies are our economic engine and provide many jobs. We must roll out the proverbial red carpet for our businesses.

133748.explanation: There is no need for stricter enforcement; there should just be enforcement according to laws and regulations. We should collaborate with well-intentioned companies to keep pollution as low as possible (lower than required!). Deliberate violators must be dealt with firmly.

133749.explanation: Our industry must have sufficient space. We prefer to give any new housing, sports, and recreation more room in nature, as long as this is in the interest of our Zaan residents.

133750.explanation: Disagree: our Zaan youths deserve priority; they have to wait a long time for housing (which is a right!) and even postpone starting a family. Madness!

133751.explanation: Given the current housing shortage and pressure on our facilities (impending shortage of GPs, crowded public spaces, traffic congestion, shortage of enforcement, etc.), we should not do more than we can handle.

133752.explanation: This encourages dumping and pollution. In theory, the idea sounds good, but in practice it causes problems. We focus more on post-sorting when that works best in practice.

133753.explanation: What is sustainable in this case? Who decides? We must focus on attracting companies that fit within our Zaan environment and residents. Naturally, we support minimizing pollution as much as possible and are committed to cooperating to reduce it.

133754.explanation: We prefer cows and grass in the fields over solar panels, but we are definitely not in favor of banning sustainability initiatives when they can support themselves. As far as we are concerned, solar panels belong on the roofs.

133756.explanation: We are green, but not crazy! Goals must be realistic and achievable. Unachievable and/or unaffordable goals do not fit responsible governance.

133757.explanation: In other European countries, residents receive subsidies to actually stay on gas. We support sustainability, but moving away from gas should not be an end in itself, especially considering the problems with electricity supply (grid congestion) which will remain an issue for years. National legislation targets 2050.

133758.explanation: Wind turbines run on subsidies and, besides producing expensive electricity, mainly cause nuisance to people and the environment.

133759.explanation: Our municipality seems to be the dumping ground for the region. We have to wait half a century for a highway connection, if it ever comes, but a high-voltage power line is forced upon us in record time.

133760.explanation: Schiphol is a major economic engine for our country and is vital for our business climate. The nuisance from (night) flights must be consistently addressed to improve our livability, which is already under sufficient pressure.

133761.explanation: (Sports) clubs are the glue of our society. The club life promotes health, connection, quality of life, etc. With the decline in volunteers, it is increasingly difficult for clubs to pay their bills. They deserve our support for their important work.

133763.explanation: Both small and large events are important, and currently there are too few places where events can be organized. Of course, good participation and agreements with the surroundings (such as neighbors and businesses) must be ensured.

133764.explanation: Outdoor sports are booming and are a fantastic way to exercise in the fresh air and come together. Sport is one of the best investments there is!

133765.explanation: Many of our fields and buildings are too crowded, including the swimming pools, and do not fully meet current standards. A healthy municipality provides adequate facilities for associations at a social tariff. This also includes enough affordable pool water for swimming clubs.

133766.explanation: Heritage is important; it tells the story of our Zaan region’s history. It is our authenticity. Much heritage has already been demolished in the past, which we must try to prevent as much as possible in the future. However, we think it is very important to discuss this well together with the owners of the buildings and offer assistance with costs and possibilities for use and sustainability. This may also mean that not every historic building must necessarily be a monument, have a defining status, or other protection, but where developments are possible, that also helps.

133767.explanation: As long as there is a shortage of police capacity, enforcement officers (BOAs), who have received more powers in recent years, can take over tasks. We have an enforcement shortage and that must be addressed.

133768.explanation: In risk areas there must be comprehensive supervision. Unfortunately, the municipal council voted against a proposal from us, but we will not drop this!

133770.explanation: Even if we only keep a few young people on the right track with this, our youth are the future. We must continue to invest in this.

133771.explanation: Yes, both in repression (action against nuisance) and in prevention (prevention by offering alternatives such as sports and exercise).

133772.explanation: 5,000 extra homes in Krommenie and Wormerveer is too many. We will have to find other solutions for the housing shortage. Krommenie and Wormerveer cannot handle such numbers. Livability, accessibility, and the historic character are at stake (priority does not apply here).

133773.explanation: That is the case everywhere. The Netherlands is already a worldwide leader in social rental housing, but this does not solve the problem. The issue lies in the lack of flow in the housing market, the high influx, and the shortage of affordable housing (mid-priced owner-occupied and rental).

133774.explanation: The municipality should, in principle, not get involved here. Still, we see the value in the free removal of tiles. Destoning gardens improves drainage. The inability of rainwater to drain properly is increasingly becoming a problem.

133775.explanation: Demolition is certainly not always the best solution. Experts indicate that an existing building can often still be well renovated and adapted. Moreover, much has already been demolished in the past, and we do not want to be a municipality of 'block boxes'.

133776.explanation: Having the much-needed facilities is, in our view, a basic condition for new residential areas, together with good infrastructure. No more 'building just to build,' which primarily harms existing residents, but building with vision.

133777.explanation: Although subdividing homes into multiple units is logical, it adds further pressure on our traffic problems, parking issues, lack of facilities, etc.

133778.explanation: Our residents are also our heritage and our youth is our future. Naturally, we must help as many people as possible where we can, but not at the expense of our residents. The municipality is precisely responsible for that care!

133779.explanation: That is the case everywhere. The Netherlands is already a worldwide leader in social rental housing, but this does not solve the problem. The problem lies in the lack of flow in the housing market, the high influx, and the shortage of affordable housing. Also, plans with a lot of social rental housing are financially not feasible.

133780.explanation: It does not help at all in solving the housing shortage. Sometimes it can help when, for example, parents can buy a house for their children. Besides, there is also a large shortage of rental homes, and these also become much scarcer.

133781.explanation: There is already a shortage of business locations. We want to maintain and preferably expand employment in the municipality by using our industrial sites more efficiently.

133782.explanation: Agreed, especially for the residents of those villages and in line with the history of the respective village.

133783.explanation: Money alone does not solve this problem. It is about having enough work, enough opportunities, and also the willingness to work. Unfortunately, working (more) does not always pay off in the Netherlands, so residents sometimes lose money by working (more).

133784.explanation: It is essential that everyone continues to participate and remains connected to our community. No one benefits from sitting at home alone.

133785.explanation: Especially in Wormerland, one bridge is no longer sufficient. The Zaan region has grown too much for this. The bridge must be part of a broader integrated mobility plan for the North. Without the A8-A9 connection, the N203 will become completely congested.

133786.explanation: Agreed, PROVIDED the Westerkoog bypass is made suitable for this! The residents of Westerkoog deserve a safe environment, and this must be fully taken into account.

133787.explanation: Our roads are already congested and the municipality keeps building. We support realistic construction plans with associated parking facilities and roads for good traffic flow.

133788.explanation: It is madness that a different decision was made. Our railway line also serves many other municipalities. When it comes to the wish for an underground railway, the province is unresponsive, but a high-voltage line is quickly pushed through. Our residents would greatly benefit from an underground railway!

133790.explanation: For a relatively low amount, we can connect to other initiatives.

133791.explanation: Our residents of Krommenie and Assendelft also deserve clean air and a good connection. The enormous daily traffic and traffic jams create an unhealthy living environment and much traffic nuisance. The national government must keep its word and deliver! In the meantime, we must focus on alternatives.

133792.explanation: The accessibility of both Poelenburg and the Achtersluispolder improves with the opening of De Vlinder.

133793.explanation: With the current housing crisis, our children continue living at home longer. Parking is already a problem; it is madness to reduce the number of parking spaces further.

133794.explanation: Paid parking must remain affordable and used only where strictly necessary as a tool. Paid parking should not be an excuse to fill the coffers.

133795.explanation: The entire municipality of Zaanstad must NOT become an environmental zone. Not only is this impractical due to grid congestion, it also yields little and mainly imposes costs on our entrepreneurs. Additionally, our residents will pay more for companies that only drive electrically, which we already see happening elsewhere.

133796.explanation: Agreed where this adds value to traffic safety such as in residential areas and situations without separate bicycle paths, but this is not the case everywhere in every situation. Tailored solutions in consultation with residents is our motto here.

133797.explanation: A rich and attractive club life already catches and engages many young people; we cannot invest enough in this. 'Other' youths must also be able to meet each other well and safely without this harming the surroundings.

133798.explanation: Every village and neighborhood must have the necessary facilities, not just a care community center. Responding to the combination of facilities, making them better accessible and easier to find, also helps keep them affordable.

133799.explanation: Informal caregivers are not a cost item but vital in our aging society. We cannot thank them enough for all their hard work and the love they generally provide.

133800.explanation: Nothing is truly free; in this case, free means others pay for it. The care for pregnant women in Zaanstad is generally well arranged.

133801.explanation: This statement does not cover it. Every child in Zaanstad must be water-safe! This is possible because swimming lessons are financially accessible, and they are; we already have a scheme for this. We support including swimming education within physical education, focused on water safety.

statements.title: There should be a permanent event venue in Zaanstad North.
statements.pro: A fixed site makes it easier to organize events. With an appropriate permanent venue, some parties believe events have less impact on the local area.
statements.con: According to some parties, it's a waste of space to reserve a single site in Zaanstad North exclusively for events. Using different locations is better because the nuisance is spread across the municipality.

statements.title: The municipality should enforce stricter controls on pollution by companies in Zaanstad.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality is responsible for monitoring pollution caused by companies in Zaanstad. The municipality has this monitoring carried out by the North Sea Canal Area Environmental Service. That agency checks in Zaanstad whether companies comply with rules on, for example, emissions.
statements.pro: A clean living environment is important for the health of Zaanstad's residents. Stricter enforcement is important to protect nature and the environment.
statements.con: Supervision should be proportionate to the pollution. Therefore inspections should follow existing laws and regulations and not be stricter. According to some parties, there is no indication that stricter controls are necessary.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.moreInfo: Property tax (ozb) is an annual tax you pay to the municipality. You pay it if you own or use a house, building, or plot of land. The amount of tax depends on the value of the property (WOZ value) that you own or use. The municipality ultimately decides how much ozb residents must pay.
statements.pro: The property tax (ozb) is not intended to plug financial gaps in the municipality's budget. Some parties think the ozb in Zaanstad is high.
statements.con: If residents pay less property tax (ozb), the municipality has less money for services, for example in culture, green spaces and care. Some parties say Zaanstad's property tax is not high.

statements.title: For important decisions, the municipality should ask residents for their opinion via a referendum.

titleParts.text: Bij belangrijke besluiten moet de gemeente de inwoners om hun mening vragen via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.moreInfo: In a referendum, a government presents a single question to voters. They can vote for or against. In a municipal referendum all residents of Zaanstad aged 16 or older may vote. Both the municipal council and ordinary citizens can request a referendum. If citizens want a referendum, they must collect signatures from others. The municipality’s rules state how many signatures are required. In the Netherlands the result of a referendum is not binding, meaning the government does not have to follow it and can make a different decision.
statements.pro: This involves residents in important decisions that affect their living environment. A referendum is democratic. According to some parties, a referendum can help find good solutions to issues.
statements.con: Referendums oversimplify complex choices. Residents already vote every four years for a party and are thus sufficiently represented in the municipal council when decisions are made. According to some parties, it's too expensive and complicated to hold a referendum for every important decision.

statements.title: In new construction in Zaanstad the emphasis should be on mid-range rental and owner-occupied housing.
statements.moreInfo: Mid-priced homes are rental or owner-occupied homes priced just above the social threshold, intended for middle-income households who earn too much for social rent but cannot afford an expensive home.
statements.pro: A problem in the housing market is the lack of turnover. Mid-priced rental homes are needed so tenants in social housing can move on, freeing up their social rental homes. If there are more mid-priced houses for sale, seniors will move out of cheaper owner-occupied homes and those homes will become available.
statements.con: Zaanstad primarily needs more affordable housing, because the housing shortage is greatest among people seeking social rental housing. A good mix of cheap and mid-priced homes and social rental housing is essential.

statements.title: Residents of Zaanstad should start paying per use for underground waste containers.
statements.moreInfo: At the moment, recyclables (for example, old paper and organic waste) are collected separately in the municipality of Zaanstad. Residents can throw residual waste into underground waste containers. The municipality currently does not differentiate in the annual fees residents pay between one- and multiple-person households or the number of times the underground waste containers are used.
statements.pro: According to some parties, Zaanstad produces too much residual waste. If less waste is rewarded, residents will make more effort to recycle and there will be less residual waste. It is fair that residents and businesses that produce more waste also pay more.
statements.con: This can lead residents to dump their waste illegally to avoid paying, resulting in litter on the streets. Some parties consider it unfair for large families and people with lots of medical waste if they have to pay more.

statements.title: Zaanstad should not take in more asylum seekers than it is legally obliged to. The municipality currently does.

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision.

titleParts.text: opvangen dan waartoe de gemeente wettelijk verplicht is. Dat doet de gemeente nu wel.

statements.moreInfo: Zaanstad is sheltering 1,550 asylum seekers, while the target under the Distribution Act is 818. That is the number the municipality must receive from the national government. 1,550 is more, but there is a reason. Municipalities in the Zaanstreek-Waterland region have agreements to share the reception of asylum seekers. They agreed that Zaanstad will provide the asylum reception. In return, the municipality of Purmerend works to meet the target for Ukrainian refugees, and the smaller municipalities take in unaccompanied minor asylum seekers.
statements.pro: If the municipality does more than its legal duty, that comes at the expense of residents because it puts pressure on services. According to some parties, accommodating more asylum seekers also means more nuisance. It's time for other municipalities to take responsibility.
statements.con: It is social and humane for Zaanstad to take in more asylum seekers than required. The shelters cause no nuisance, some parties say, so there is no reason to accept fewer asylum seekers. According to some parties, companies are very happy with the workers coming from the asylum shelters.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent houses from being converted into multiple separate rooms (house splitting).

titleParts.glossary: Dividing a home into smaller rooms to rent each room separately.

statements.moreInfo: If an owner wants to rent out or sell a home as separate rooms to different people, the municipality of Zaanstad must grant a permit. The municipality decides whether to issue that permit and under what conditions it may be allowed, for example only for homes of a certain size.
statements.pro: Converting houses into multiple rental rooms causes nuisance for neighbors.
statements.con: Subdividing houses into multiple rooms increases available housing, for example for young people.

statements.title: The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality can only decide to a limited extent whether solar panels may be installed on agricultural land. Provincial and national rules apply to this.
statements.pro: Sustainable energy generation helps meet climate goals. Solar panels help alleviate the electricity shortage.
statements.con: Solar panels on agricultural land harm the landscape. Agricultural land is intended for farmers who produce food.

statements.title: The municipality should invest in a night train between Zaanstad and Amsterdam.
statements.pro: A night train makes it safer to travel from Amsterdam to Zaanstad at night. A night train is relatively inexpensive, some parties say. After all, there is already a night train between Amsterdam and Alkmaar that would just need to stop in Zaanstad.
statements.con: A night train is not necessary because a night bus already runs every hour with many stops. According to some parties, it is expensive to have a night train stop in Zaanstad.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate funds for better clubhouses and playing fields for sports clubs.
statements.moreInfo: Through the “Participate in Sport” subsidy scheme, sports clubs can apply to the municipality of Zaanstad for funding. For example, for maintenance and renovation of their fields.
statements.pro: Sport and physical activity are healthy.
statements.con: Sports clubs should be financially self-sufficient.

statements.title: Zaanstad should deploy more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.moreInfo: The municipality is responsible for enforcement of minor offenses, for example parking without paying, cycling on the pavement or littering in nature. People who do this receive a fine. The municipality's enforcement officers are boa's (special investigation officers). Police officers are general investigation officers and have different tasks and powers. There are also boa's who do not work for the municipality, for example on trains.
statements.pro: Municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) help make the city safer. There are too few police officers on the street, say some parties. BOAs can help ensure sufficient enforcement in Zaanstad.
statements.con: According to some parties, increasing safety is not achieved with BOA's (municipal enforcement officers); other measures are needed. Instead of more BOA's, the existing officers could be deployed differently.

statements.title: Zaanstad should offer homeowners the option to buy out their ground lease on favorable terms.

titleParts.text: Zaanstad moet huizenbezitters de mogelijkheid bieden om hun

titleParts.glossary: System in which the municipality remains the owner of the land. As a homeowner you pay rent (ground lease) to the municipality for use of the land.

statements.moreInfo: Owner-occupied homes are not always on privately owned land. Sometimes the land is owned by someone else, usually the municipality. People who buy such a house are leaseholders and must pay annually for the right to use the land. That annual payment is called the ground lease fee (erfpachtcanon). Sometimes the homeowner can buy out the ground lease fee for several years. If they sell the house, the new owner does not have to pay the ground lease fee again until that bought-out period ends.
statements.pro: Leasehold creates uncertainty and financial stress for homeowners.
statements.con: If the municipality of Zaanstad holds houses on leasehold, the municipality has more control over that land.

statements.title: Industry in Zaanstad should be allowed to expand into areas that are currently natural areas.
statements.moreInfo: At the moment, expansion of industrial areas in Zaanstad is being considered within existing industrial zones. Examples of these are HoogTij and the Achtersluispolder.
statements.pro: Industry provides jobs, including for residents of Zaanstad.
statements.con: Nature makes a city livable and therefore must be protected.

statements.title: The municipality should designate as many historic buildings as possible as monuments.
statements.moreInfo: A monument is a government-protected building, site, or object that has historical, architectural, or scientific value. There are national, municipal, and provincial monuments. For almost any change to a protected monument, the owner needs a permit.
statements.pro: Heritage is important because historic buildings tell the story of our Zaan region's history. Many special buildings were demolished in the past; you prevent that by giving buildings monument status. Protecting historic buildings as monuments makes Zaanstad more livable for residents.
statements.con: A historic building can be preserved without being given monument status. Monuments cost the municipality and owners a lot of money. Less may be changed in a monument, while buildings sometimes need to be altered or replaced by new construction to meet modern requirements.

statements.title: Zaanstad should expand camera surveillance in the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.moreInfo: In Zaanstad there are currently cameras in the hospitality area of Zaandam (including the Dam and immediate surroundings), partly around Zaandam station, and some at Krommenie-Assendelft station. The cameras were placed by the municipality for public order and safety. That means the municipality can use the cameras for monitoring. They are not investigative cameras placed by the Public Prosecution Service to detect crimes.
statements.pro: Camera surveillance increases safety, say some parties. According to them, cameras help catch criminals.
statements.con: According to some parties, camera surveillance only provides a false sense of security. Camera surveillance infringes on the privacy of Zaanstad residents.

statements.title: Residents of Zaanstad who are expecting a child must be able to receive a free pregnancy course from the municipality.
statements.moreInfo: A pregnancy course usually consists of several evening sessions. At those sessions expectant parents receive information about pregnancy, giving birth, and parenthood. Sometimes such a course is reimbursed by additional health insurance. The Municipal Public Health Service (GGD) already offers a free pregnancy course in Zaanstad for people on a minimum income.
statements.pro: This will encourage more expectant parents to prepare well for having a child. This contributes to equal starting opportunities for all children in Zaanstad. A good start in life is essential for children to develop well.
statements.con: Free pregnancy courses are often already offered by the municipal health service (GGD). Some self-reliance can be expected from residents, and expectant parents in Zaanstad can already access information about pregnancy and parenthood. Some parties consider this meddling.

statements.title: The municipality must allow buildings to be demolished and replaced with something new.
statements.moreInfo: In Zaanstad it is currently permitted to demolish buildings and construct new ones. Exceptions to this are monuments, landmark buildings, and city or village views (protected townscapes).
statements.pro: Demolition is sometimes necessary to build something that meets modern requirements. By tearing down old buildings and constructing new homes, more houses can be built.
statements.con: Renovation is better because it preserves characteristic buildings. Renovating is more sustainable than demolishing.

statements.title: About 40% of the newly built homes in Zaanstad must be social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Zaanstad moet ongeveer 40% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home that at the start of the tenancy is not more expensive than a certain amount. In 2026 that is €932.93 per month.

statements.moreInfo: At the moment 30% of the homes in the municipality of Zaanstad are social rental homes. That is roughly the same as the national percentage.
statements.pro: Zaanstad needs more social rental homes because housing need is highest among people seeking social housing. Building more social rental homes contributes, according to some parties, to well-mixed neighborhoods.
statements.con: Zaanstad has a relatively large number of low-income residents, which puts pressure on services. The problem in the housing market is a lack of turnover. To solve the long waiting lists for social rental housing, more mid-range rental housing is needed.

statements.title: The municipality must provide young people with information about the dangers of possessing and using knives and other stabbing weapons.
statements.pro: It is important to prevent young people, who experience a lot of social pressure, from using knives for crime or violence. Education helps with that, say some parties.
statements.con: The possession of certain stabbing weapons is prohibited and the use of such weapons for violence or crime is punishable. Therefore the emphasis should be on confiscating stabbing weapons and imposing penalties.

statements.title: People who receive social assistance benefits must, in return, do socially useful work or take part in training.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have too little income or assets.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.moreInfo: People who have no work and no other benefit can apply to the municipality for social assistance. In some municipalities they are required to perform a counter-performance. They then do socially useful work for which they are not paid.
statements.pro: This helps people stay involved in society and find paid work. Some parties think it's reasonable for recipients of social assistance to give something back.
statements.con: Not everyone is able to do social work or undertake training. Mandatory requirements increase stress and distrust.

statements.title: The Zaans Climate Agreement states that the municipality must be climate-neutral by 2040. Zaanstad must make an effort to reach that goal earlier.

titleParts.text: In het Zaans Klimaatakkoord staat dat de gemeente in 2040

titleParts.glossary: A situation in which the municipality as a whole does not make climate change worse, for example by limiting CO2 emissions.

titleParts.text: moet zijn. Zaanstad moet zich inspannen om dat doel eerder te halen.

statements.moreInfo: An energy- or climate-neutral municipality produces as much clean energy as its residents and businesses consume. The Zaans Climate Agreement aims to be climate-neutral between 2030 and 2040. To achieve this, Zaanstad must reduce emissions of CO2 and other greenhouse gases, such as from gas, electricity, and fuel use. Residents also cause indirect emissions through greenhouse gases associated with goods, travel, and homes. But most emissions come from industry. Zaanstad estimates that, at the current pace, emissions will be halved by 2040. The goal to be climate-neutral by then is a challenge.
statements.pro: Zaanstad should become climate neutral as soon as possible because of the climate crisis. This is good for residents' living environment. According to some parties, a climate-neutral municipality is beneficial for residents' finances.
statements.con: Zaanstad's effect on the global climate crisis is negligible. According to some parties, it is not feasible to become climate-neutral earlier than 2040. According to some parties, it would come at the expense of residents' finances.

statements.title: The Vlinderbrug between Poelenburg and the Achtersluispolder in Zaandam must be opened to cars.
statements.moreInfo: The bridge between Poelenburg and the Achtersluispolder is intended for public transport buses. Cyclists and pedestrians can also use it.
statements.pro: Cars can drive faster and more smoothly through Zaandam South if they are allowed to use the Vlinderbrug. This increases safety.
statements.con: This creates extra cut-through traffic. The bridge is intended for public transport, cyclists and pedestrians. They suffer if they have to share the bridge with cars.

statements.title: Anyone who buys a home in our municipality must live in it themselves.
statements.moreInfo: The national government makes it possible for municipalities to introduce a purchase restriction for owner-occupied homes up to a certain price. Someone who buys such a home must live in it themselves. Only in exceptional cases is it then possible to rent out the house, for example if parents buy a home for their child. Zaanstad has already introduced a purchase restriction for homes up to €420,000, the national affordability threshold.
statements.pro: Some parties believe: houses are for living in, not for profit. This creates a fairer housing market. Homebuyers then don't have to outbid investors who push up prices.
statements.con: People may decide for themselves what to do with their homes; the government does not interfere. Buying to rent out can help alleviate the housing shortage, because there is also a shortage of rental homes.

statements.title: Paid parking in Zaanstad must be made cheaper.
statements.pro: The municipality should not use paid parking to fill the coffers. Zaanstad should be a welcoming municipality. Parking is a basic service.
statements.con: Parking nuisance will then increase. Parking rates in Zaanstad are not high compared with other cities. People should be encouraged not to take the car.

statements.title: The municipality must invest more money in combating poverty.
statements.pro: Living in poverty causes health problems and stress. Living in poverty leads to debt and social exclusion. The municipality has a responsibility to help the vulnerable in society.
statements.con: According to some parties, there is no evidence that more money for poverty reduction is needed. More money does not have to go to poor residents; funds should be spent more effectively. The municipality would be better off investing in preventing poverty, for example by investing more in education.

statements.title: The 'appreciation' payment for informal caregivers must increase from 50 to 140 euros per year.

titleParts.glossary: Someone who provides unpaid and often long-term care for sick family members or friends.

titleParts.text: moet omhoog van 50 naar 140 euro per jaar.

statements.moreInfo: The municipality of Zaanstad gives caregivers an annual token of appreciation for their service, the “mantelzorgwaardering.” Caregivers aged 18 and over receive €50 and caregivers aged 6 to 17 receive a voucher of €30. This applies to residents of Zaanstad who provide at least 6 hours of care per week for three months or 250 hours per year for someone in their environment, and who do not receive payment for it.
statements.pro: Informal caregivers are important in our aging society. Therefore they should be better compensated. Informal caregivers save the municipality money by easing pressure on professional caregivers.
statements.con: Informal caregivers don't need money; they need rest. The municipality should spend extra on respite care, such as day programs, which relieve caregivers. Increasing from €50 to €140 per year does not show greater appreciation for caregivers.

statements.title: The municipality must allow building on the edges of the current villages.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality - together with the province - decides on building permits, expansions, or changes of use in the Zaan landscape. There is increasing demand for expansions and new construction, also on the edges of villages. Zaanstad wants to build many houses. But where is that appropriate and where absolutely not? How will the special character of the landscape be preserved? The municipality has partial responsibility, but the province also has policies. In valuable provincial landscapes the province only allows small expansions.
statements.pro: Zaanstad needs more housing and available space is limited.
statements.con: The green areas around Zaanstad must be protected.

statements.title: People with a residence permit must be given priority for social rental housing in the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home that at the start of the tenancy is not more expensive than a certain amount. In 2026 that is €932.93 per month.

statements.moreInfo: People who flee their country because of war or personal persecution can apply for asylum in the Netherlands. The Immigration and Naturalisation Service (IND) assesses their application and decides whether they may remain in the Netherlands. If the IND decides a refugee may stay, that person receives a residence status. These people are therefore also called status holders. Every six months municipalities receive a target from the national government to house a number of status holders. Municipalities are free to do this with or without priority status. At the moment about 8% of the housing supply in Zaanstad goes to status holders.
statements.pro: Every vulnerable person who urgently needs housing deserves priority. According to some parties, in the Zaan region it is not that common for social rental homes to go to status holders, so giving priority is acceptable.
statements.con: Status holders should be treated the same as others, which means no priority. Not status holders, but Zaanstad's young people deserve priority.

statements.title: An extra bridge must be built over the Zaan.
statements.moreInfo: Currently there are several bridges over the Zaan, including the Zaanbrug. With the construction of thousands of new houses in places like Wormer, Wormerveer, and Krommenie, car traffic will increase. Therefore an additional bridge over the Zaan is being considered by both the municipality of Zaanstad and the municipality of Wormerland. There is no concrete plan yet, but the bridge would be between Wormer and Wormerveer.
statements.pro: Traffic is congested, so an extra bridge is needed. Better traffic flow is safer.
statements.con: An additional bridge over the Zaan is not necessary. It does not need to be made even easier for cars in Zaanstad.

shootoutStatements.title: Only companies that operate sustainably should be allowed to establish themselves in our municipality from now on.
Zaanstad should establish neighborhood and village councils that are always allowed to have a say in the municipality’s plans.
The municipality should allocate more funds to tackle youth nuisance in Zaandam East and Zaanstad North.
By 2040 all homes in Zaanstad must be off the gas network.
When allocating a social rental home, applicants from the municipality should be given priority as much as possible.
More wind turbines should be installed in the municipality.
More outdoor sports facilities should be created in Zaanstad.
The Bus Bridge De Binding, which connects Westerkoog and Westerwatering, should be open 24 hours a day.
Zaanstad should oppose the installation of high-voltage pylons in the Assendelft and Westzaan polders.
Zaanstad should call on the national government and the province to proceed with building the A8/A9 connection.
Zaanstad should put more pressure on the national government to end night flights to and from Schiphol.
Fewer than the planned 5,000 new homes should be built in Krommenie and Wormerveer.
More business parks should be created in Zaanstad.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.
Every new residential area should have facilities such as a school, a GP and childcare as soon as people move in.
The municipality should reward residents who make their gardens green and remove paving.
Housing should also be built on business parks.
The municipality should not build any additional roads for cars.
The Zaan Line rail should be partly put underground.
The entire municipality of Zaanstad should become a low-emission zone.
Every village in the municipality of Zaanstad should get a care community center for the elderly.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.
Every village in the municipality of Zaanstad should get a meeting place for young people.
Children in Zaanstad should receive free swimming lessons.
The municipality should reduce the number of parking spaces in residential areas.
